# Create isolated language overhaul

# Files mentioned by the user:

## VOYAGEWRIGHT UNIVERSAL LANGUAGE IMPLEMENTATION Complete Product-Wide Language M…: C:\Users\kgray\.codex/attachments/27279cdf-3ea9-428e-8cdd-3c6c6b764477/pasted-text.txt

## My request for Codex:
# ADDENDUM: ISOLATED LANGUAGE-OVERHAUL WORKTREE

Perform this Universal Language Overhaul in a **separate Git worktree and dedicated branch** so that it can proceed concurrently with Project Lanternwake without modifying or disrupting the active Lanternwake worktree.

## Required setup

Before editing source:

1. Inspect the canonical repository and record:

   * repository root;
   * current branch and commit;
   * worktree list;
   * staged, unstaged and untracked files;
   * active Lanternwake branch and worktree.

2. Create or use a dedicated branch:

```text
development/universal-language
```

3. Create or use a sibling worktree dedicated exclusively to this overhaul, for example:

```text
Forever-Treasure-Language
```

4. Base the language branch on the newest safe committed project state. Do not copy the repository manually.

5. Do not modify, reset, clean, stash, switch, merge into or otherwise interfere with the active Lanternwake worktree or branch.

Never use destructive commands such as:

```text
git reset --hard
git clean
git checkout -- .
git restore .
git stash --all
force push
```

## Authority boundaries

Treat:

```text
Project Lanternwake = structural and animation authority
Universal Language Overhaul = terminology and product-copy authority
```

Lanternwake retains ownership of:

* component and scene architecture;
* animation runtimes;
* scene hosts;
* lifecycle behavior;
* transitions;
* motion accessibility;
* presentation mechanics.

This branch owns:

* visible product language;
* terminology;
* headings;
* navigation labels;
* buttons;
* instructions;
* empty states;
* errors;
* notifications;
* accessibility wording;
* website, application and connector copy;
* centralized copy catalogs;
* detection and removal of obsolete language.

Do not undo structural changes merely to make wording easier to apply.

## Concurrent-change protection

Before editing any file:

1. Determine whether Lanternwake is also likely to modify it.
2. Prefer centralized language catalogs and narrowly scoped text changes.
3. Preserve all current component behavior and animation hooks.
4. Do not refactor animation or layout architecture unless strictly required for language centralization.
5. Commit work in small, coherent batches.

Where practical, begin with lower-conflict work:

* language inventory;
* terminology source of truth;
* typed copy catalogs;
* prohibited legacy-language scanner;
* documentation;
* copy-focused tests;
* website and connector wording outside active Lanternwake files.

## Synchronization and merge strategy

Do not merge this branch directly into an actively changing Lanternwake branch.

At safe Lanternwake phase milestones:

1. Fetch the newest committed Lanternwake state.
2. Merge that state into `development/universal-language`.
3. Resolve conflicts in the language worktree while preserving:

   * Lanternwake’s current structure;
   * the new universal language;
   * animation hooks;
   * accessibility behavior;
   * test coverage.
4. Continue the overhaul against the updated structure.

Final combination must occur through a dedicated integration branch, for example:

```text
development/lanternwake-language-integration
```

The integration order should be:

1. current completed Lanternwake branch;
2. Universal Language branch;
3. conflict resolution;
4. complete validation;
5. merge into the canonical development branch only after validation succeeds.

## Runtime isolation

Use a different development port from Lanternwake.

Do not share mutable test databases, running servers, Playwright sessions or generated build directories between worktrees.

The language branch should use read-only fixtures or its own isolated test database whenever mutation is necessary.

## Completion report

In the final report include:

* worktree path;
* branch;
* starting and ending commits;
* Lanternwake baseline merged;
* files changed;
* centralized language catalogs created;
* legacy-language checks added;
* overlapping files encountered;
* merge conflicts resolved;
* validation commands and results;
* remaining integration risks.

Do not merge into Lanternwake automatically.

Complete the language branch, commit it cleanly and stop for integration review.

Voice Architecture\r\n\r\nThe product does not have one universal speaker.\r\n\r\nImplement and preserve six distinct language layers.\r\n\r\n4.1 Voyagewright brand voice\r\n\r\nApplies to:\r\n\r\nmarketing pages;\r\nlanding pages;\r\nproduct descriptions;\r\ntrailers;\r\nonboarding;\r\npublic product copy;\r\nbeta and waitlist pages;\r\npromotional empty states.\r\n\r\nVoice:\r\n\r\naspirational;\r\nunderstandable;\r\ninviting;\r\ncinematic without vagueness;\r\nemotionally resonant without exaggeration.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nCreate stories worth entering.\r\n\r\nStories made to be played.\r\n\r\nBuild a Chronicle. Gather your crew. Turn a familiar world into a story made for them.\r\n\r\nYour games already provide the world. Voyagewright gives that world a story written for your crew.\r\n\r\nAvoid empty marketing language such as:\r\n\r\nRevolutionary next-generation immersive platform\r\nEpic unforgettable magical journeys\r\nThe ultimate storytelling solution\r\nExperience storytelling like never before\r\n\r\nThe product must explain what it does.\r\n\r\nIt must not hide behind atmospheric fog wearing a pricing button.\r\n\r\n4.2 Neutral platform voice\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx\nATTACHMENT_261_780\r\n\r\nApplies to:\r\n\r\nauthentication;\r\naccount management;\r\nnavigation;\r\ninvitations;\r\nsettings;\r\nnotifications;\r\nsystem status;\r\nsynchronization;\r\ngeneral application state.\r\n\r\nVoice:\r\n\r\nconcise;\r\nhuman;\r\nlightly atmospheric only where useful;\r\nnever pretending to be a Chronicle narrator.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nInvitation accepted.\r\n\r\nYour Voyage is ready.\r\n\r\nProgress saved.\r\n\r\nThe next Chapter has not been released.\r\n\r\nNo active Voyages.\r\n\r\nReturn to the Chronicle Library.\r\n\r\nThe platform must not falsely say:\r\n\r\nI have prepared your next clue.\r\nI have been waiting for you.\r\nI remember your journey.\r\n\r\nunless a specific authored narrator or character owns that statement.\r\n\r\n4.3 Chronicle narrator voice\r\n\r\nApplies only to Chronicle-authored content.\r\n\r\nThis may include:\r\n\r\nnarration;\r\njournals;\r\nletters;\r\ncharacter dialogue;\r\nfound documents;\r\nriddles;\r\nauthored hints;\r\nstory-specific instructions;\r\nstory-specific confirmations.\r\n\r\nThe Chronicle narrator is not the Voyagewright platform.\r\n\r\nEach Chronicle may have its own:\r\n\r\ngenre;\r\ntone;\r\nnarrator;\r\ncharacters;\r\nhumor;\r\nvocabulary;\r\nemotional rhythm.\r\n\r\nDo not rewrite authored Chronicle prose into the generic Voyagewright platform voice unless it is clearly placeholder or sample content included for product demonstration.\r\n\r\nDo not force nautical language into non-nautical Chronicles.\r\n\r\nA science-fiction Chronicle must not sound like an old sailor gained unauthorized access to the airlock controls.\r\n\r\n4.4 Player experience voice\r\n\r\nApplies to:\r\n\r\nChapter-release presentations;\r\nWaypoint reveals;\r\nArtifact recovery;\r\nEcho discovery;\r\nJournal state;\r\nplayer progression;\r\nwaiting states;\r\ncollections;\r\nplayer-facing recovery.\r\n\r\nUse the pattern:\r\n\r\nFunctional truth first. Atmospheric meaning second.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nChapter III released\r\nA new entry has opened in the journal.\r\n\r\nArtifact recovered\r\nThe sea-glass lens has been added to your collection.\r\n\r\nA new Echo was found\r\nSome places keep more than treasure.\r\n\r\nWaypoint revealed\r\nA new bearing has been marked on the chart.\r\n\r\nThe first line communicates system truth.\r\n\r\nThe second line creates atmosphere.\r\n\r\nDo not make the player decipher whether the system worked.\r\n\r\n4.5 Captainâ€™s Console voice\r\n\r\nApplies to:\r\n\r\nChapter release controls;\r\nplayer approvals;\r\nhint delivery;\r\nverification review;\r\nsession recovery;\r\nplayer connection state;\r\nlive Voyage controls;\r\nprogression actions;\r\nCaptain-only notes and warnings.\r\n\r\nVoice:\r\n\r\noperational;\r\ndirect;\r\nconcise;\r\ncalm;\r\nsuitable for use during a live event.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nRelease Chapter III\r\n\r\nApprove discovery\r\n\r\nSend Hint 2\r\n\r\nPause Voyage\r\n\r\nEnd Voyage\r\n\r\nAwaiting player confirmation\r\n\r\nThe player is offline. This action will be delivered when they reconnect.\r\n\r\nThis action advances the entire Crew to the next Passage.\r\n\r\nAvoid decorative labels such as:\r\n\r\nUnseal the mysteries of Chapter III\r\nBestow the second whisper\r\nCommand the tide to turn\r\nGrant passage through the veil\r\n\r\nThe Captain may be running the experience, speaking to players, navigating, monitoring several devices, and being shot at by skeletons. The interface should not demand literary interpretation at that exact moment.\r\n\r\n4.6 Voyagewright Studio voice\r\n\r\nApplies to:\r\n\r\ncreation;\r\nediting;\r\nvalidation;\r\nversioning;\r\npublishing;\r\nasset management;\r\npreview;\r\nstructure;\r\nlogic;\r\ncontent organization.\r\n\r\nVoice:\r\n\r\ncreator-to-creator;\r\nencouraging;\r\nstructurally intelligent;\r\nprecise;\r\nnonjudgmental;\r\ncomfortable using ordinary software and design language.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nCreate Chronicle\r\n\r\nAdd Chapter\r\n\r\nAdd Passage\r\n\r\nPreview from here\r\n\r\nDraft saved\r\n\r\nPublishing creates a permanent version.\r\n\r\nExisting Voyages will continue using their current version.\r\n\r\nChapter IV cannot be published because it has no completion path.\r\n\r\nThis Artifact is used in three published versions.\r\n\r\nDo not replace technical clarity with fantasy decoration.\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nVersion history\r\nValidation errors\r\nPublish Chronicle\r\nDraft settings\r\nAsset library\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\nEchoes of former manuscripts\r\nSend into legend\r\nMysteries preventing ascension\r\nThe creatorâ€™s sacred archive\r\n5. Fundamental Writing Rules\r\n\r\nThese rules are mandatory.\r\n\r\n5.1 Clear before clever\r\n\r\nEvery line must communicate its purpose before adding atmosphere.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nAwaiting the Captain\r\nYour progress is saved. The next Chapter will appear when it is released.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nThe tide has not yet turned.\r\nPerhaps the keeper of the Voyage still ponders what lies beyond.\r\n\r\nAtmosphere may support meaning.\r\n\r\nAtmosphere may not replace meaning.\r\n\r\n5.2 Suggest wonder instead of announcing it\r\n\r\nAvoid repeatedly describing content as:\r\n\r\nepic\r\nmagical\r\nimmersive\r\nincredible\r\nunforgettable\r\nbreathtaking\r\nlegendary\r\nemotional\r\ncinematic\r\n\r\nThose reactions must be earned by the experience.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nA new mark has appeared on the chart.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nAn incredible magical discovery has been revealed!\r\n5.3 Nautical texture, not pirate cosplay\r\n\r\nAllowed platform vocabulary includes:\r\n\r\nVoyage\r\nChronicle\r\nCaptain\r\nCrew\r\nChart\r\nCourse\r\nPassage\r\nHarbor\r\nLogbook\r\nWaypoint\r\nArtifact\r\nEcho\r\nLantern\r\nArchive\r\n\r\nAvoid fake pirate dialect in platform-owned language:\r\n\r\nArrr\r\nMatey\r\nLandlubber\r\nYe\r\nAvast\r\nMe hearties\r\nPlunder yer settings\r\nHoist yer preferences\r\nSail back to yer account\r\n\r\nChronicle-authored characters may use such language if appropriate.\r\n\r\nThe platform may not.\r\n\r\n5.4 Emotion is observed, not commanded\r\n\r\nDo not prescribe emotion.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nThe second handwriting ends here.\r\nThe remaining pages are his alone.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nThis heartbreaking discovery fills you with sadness.\r\n\r\nUse concrete evidence, behavior, objects, and silence.\r\n\r\nTrust the user.\r\n\r\n5.5 Never shame the player\r\n\r\nHints, wrong answers, failed verification, reconnection, and recovery must preserve dignity.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nThat answer does not fit the markings yet.\r\nReview the eastern symbol and try again.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nIncorrect. Try harder.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nNeed a clearer bearing?\r\nReveal the next hint.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nStuck already?\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nThe object could not be confirmed.\r\nAdjust the view so the full marker is visible.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nVerification failed because your screenshot was invalid.\r\n5.6 Humor is restrained and situational\r\n\r\nAppropriate places:\r\n\r\nharmless empty states;\r\nonboarding;\r\ncreator guidance;\r\ncertain sample content;\r\nnoncritical validation;\r\nlow-stakes loading states;\r\noptional details.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nNo Chronicles found.\r\nEven legends have to be written first.\r\n\r\nThis Chapter has no ending yet.\r\nA bold structural choice, though probably not an intentional one.\r\n\r\nHumor must not appear in:\r\n\r\naccount security;\r\nprivacy;\r\nbilling;\r\npassword reset;\r\ndata loss;\r\npermanent deletion;\r\nlegal consent;\r\nserious publishing failure;\r\nsynchronization corruption;\r\nlost progress;\r\naccessibility controls.\r\n6. Sentence-Level Standards\r\n\r\nImplement these standards across all platform-owned copy.\r\n\r\n6.1 Sentence case\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nCreate Chronicle\r\nRelease Chapter\r\nInvite Crew\r\nContinue Voyage\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\nCreate Chronicle Now\r\nCREATE CHRONICLE\r\nRELEASE CHAPTER\r\n\r\nTitle case is reserved for:\r\n\r\nproduct names;\r\nnamed interfaces;\r\nChronicle titles;\r\nChapter titles;\r\nnamed places;\r\nproper nouns.\r\n6.2 Active voice\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nThe Captain released Chapter II.\r\n\r\nAvoid:\r\n\r\nChapter II has been released by the Captain.\r\n6.3 Short action labels\r\n\r\nPrefer one to four words:\r\n\r\nBegin Voyage\r\nContinue Voyage\r\nRequest Hint\r\nOpen Journal\r\nInspect Artifact\r\nInvite Crew\r\nPublish Chronicle\r\nPreview from here\r\n6.4 Natural contractions\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nWe couldnâ€™t reconnect.\r\nThe file wasnâ€™t saved.\r\nYouâ€™re ready to continue.\r\n\r\nAvoid stiffness unless the context is formal, legal, or exceptionally serious.\r\n\r\n6.5 Exclamation marks are rare\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nSettings saved.\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\nSettings saved!!!\r\n\r\nA database write is not an Olympic event.\r\n\r\n6.6 Ellipses indicate actual ongoing work\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nReconnectingâ€¦\r\nUploadingâ€¦\r\nPreparing previewâ€¦\r\n\r\nAvoid decorative ellipses:\r\n\r\nA secret has been revealedâ€¦\r\nYour journey beginsâ€¦\r\n6.7 Direct second person\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\nYour progress is safe.\r\nChoose what should be preserved.\r\n\r\nAvoid robotic third-person phrasing:\r\n\r\nThe userâ€™s progress has been persisted.\r\n\r\nDo not seize control of the userâ€™s emotions:\r\n\r\nYou feel afraid.\r\nYou are overwhelmed.\r\nYou are deeply moved.\r\n6.8 Ordinary words remain ordinary\r\n\r\nDo not rename clear controls for thematic effect.\r\n\r\nKeep:\r\n\r\nSave\r\nCancel\r\nDelete\r\nEmail\r\nPassword\r\nPrivacy\r\nVersion history\r\nValidation errors\r\nSign out\r\nAccount\r\nDownloads\r\nSettings\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\nPreserve to the logbook\r\nAbandon course\r\nCast into the abyss\r\nSecret Captainâ€™s cipher\r\nChronicles of former revisions\r\n7. Error and Recovery Language\r\n\r\nEvery meaningful error should answer:\r\n\r\nWhat happened?\r\nWas anything lost?\r\nWhat is the system doing now?\r\nWhat can the user do next?\r\n\r\nRequired tone:\r\n\r\ncalm;\r\nspecific;\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Use Poppler rendering plus Python tools such as reportlab, pdfplumber, and pypdf for generation and extraction.\"\r\n---\r\n\r\n# PDF Skill\r\n\r\n## When To Use\r\n\r\n- Read or review PDF content where layout and visuals matter.\r\n- Create PDFs programmatically with reliable formatting.\r\n- Validate final rendering before delivery.\r\n\r\n## Workflow\r\n\r\n1. Prefer visual review: render PDF pages to PNGs and inspect them.\r\n   - Use `pdftoppm` from the bundled runtime or system Poppler when available.\r\n   - If unavailable, install Poppler or ask the user to review the output locally.\r\n2. Use `reportlab` to generate PDFs when creating new documents.\r\n3. Use `pdfplumber` or `pypdf` for text extraction and quick checks; do not rely on text extraction for layout fidelity.\r\n4. After each meaningful update, re-render pages and verify alignment, spacing, and legibility.\r\n\r\n## Temp And Output Conventions\r\n\r\n- Use `tmp/pdfs/` for intermediate files; delete them when done.\r\n- Write final artifacts under `output/pdf/` when working in this repo.\r\n- Keep filenames stable and descriptive.\r\n\r\n## Dependencies\r\n\r\nPrefer the Codex bundled workspace/runtime dependencies when available. The primary runtime is expected to include:\r\n\r\n- Python packages: `reportlab`, `pdfplumber`, `pypdf`\r\n- Rendering tools: `pdftoppm` and `pdfinfo` from Poppler\r\n\r\nIf a dependency is missing, install only what is needed.\r\n\r\nPython packages:\r\n\r\n```bash\r\nuv pip install reportlab pdfplumber pypdf\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf `uv` is unavailable:\r\n\r\n```bash\r\npython3 -m pip install reportlab pdfplumber pypdf\r\n```\r\n\r\nSystem tools for rendering:\r\n\r\n```bash\r\n# macOS (Homebrew)\r\nbrew install poppler\r\n\r\n# Ubuntu/Debian\r\nsudo apt-get install -y poppler-utils\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf installation is not possible in this environment, tell the user which dependency is missing and how to install it locally.\r\n\r\n## Environment\r\n\r\nNo required environment variables.\r\n\r\n## Rendering Command\r\n\r\n```bash\r\npdftoppm -png \"$INPUT_PDF\" \"$OUTPUT_PREFIX\"\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Quality Expectations\r\n\r\n- Maintain polished visual design: consistent typography, spacing, margins, and section hierarchy.\r\n- Avoid rendering issues: clipped text, overlapping elements, broken tables, black squares, or unreadable glyphs.\r\n- Charts, tables, and images must be sharp, aligned, and clearly labeled.\r\n- Use ASCII hyphens only. Avoid U+2011 and other Unicode dashes.\r\n- Citations and references must be human-readable; never leave tool tokens or placeholder strings.\r\n\r\n## Final Checks\r\n\r\n- Do not deliver until the latest PNG inspection shows zero visual or formatting defects.\r\n- Confirm headers, footers, page numbering, and section transitions look polished.\r\n- Keep intermediate files organized or remove them after final approval.\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.2 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n===== PAGE 1 =====\r\n\r\nABSOLUTE RELATIVE SYSTEMS\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT\r\nLanguage Design Foundation\r\nClear first. Human always. Wonder earned.\r\nVoice, tone, terminology, microcopy, error language,\r\nand narrative speaker ownership for the Voyagewright platform.\r\nFoundation v1.0  |  July 2026\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 2 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.02\r\nDocument Purpose\r\nThis document defines how Voyagewright speaks. It governs the language of the public brand, the core \r\nplatform, Player, Captain, Studio, errors, recovery states, and the boundary between platform copy and \r\nChronicle-authored narrative.\r\nIt is intended to prevent the product from slowly mutating into twelve inconsistent dialects of nautical software, \r\neach written by a different screen at two in the morning.\r\nScope\r\nThis foundation defines voice and tone principles, canonical terminology, sentence-level standards, \r\nspeaker ownership, emotional pacing, accessibility expectations, and examples for common interface \r\nstates.\r\nHow to use this document\r\n Treat the voice principles as product requirements, not optional polish.\r\n Use the canonical glossary in UI labels, documentation, database-facing display names, and marketing \r\ncopy.\r\n Keep platform language separate from Chronicle narration and character dialogue.\r\n Audit every new screen against the copy review checklist before release.\r\n Allow Chronicles to have their own voices while preserving platform clarity around them.\r\nContents\r\n1. The voice thesis\r\n2. Voice and tone\r\n3. Six governing principles\r\n4. The six voice layers\r\n5. Error and recovery language\r\n6. Canonical vocabulary\r\n7. Microcopy transformations\r\n8. Sentence-level standards\r\n9. Language across the emotional arc\r\n10. Speaker ownership\r\n11. Nautical intensity\r\n12. Accessibility and localization\r\n13. Copy governance\r\n14. Definitive voice statement\r\nAppendix A. Sample copy library\r\nAppendix B. Copy review checklist\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 3 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.03\r\n1. The Voice Thesis\r\nVoyagewright speaks with measured wonder.\r\nVoyagewright should sound like a trusted guide standing beside an unfinished map: clear enough that the user \r\nalways knows what to do, poetic enough that the interface feels like part of the experience, warm enough to feel \r\nhuman, and restrained enough never to become corny.\r\nA lantern, not a loudspeaker. A compass, not a monologue.\r\nThe voice is:\r\n Warm, but not sentimental by default.\r\n Precise, but not clinical.\r\n Observant, but not over-explanatory.\r\n Quietly imaginative, but never vague when action is required.\r\n Nautical in texture, not trapped in pirate costume.\r\n Confident, but never grandiose.\r\n Human, without pretending the system is a character unless a Chronicle explicitly provides one.\r\nThe central promise\r\nVoyagewright helps creators build stories worth entering, helps Captains guide those stories, and helps players \r\nunderstand what is happening without draining the experience of mystery.\r\nInternal shorthand\r\nClear first. Human always. Wonder earned.\r\n2. Voice and Tone\r\nThe voice remains stable. The tone responds to the moment.\r\nThe voice is the underlying personality of the product. Tone is the temporary emotional posture used for a \r\nspecific situation.\r\nContext Tone Primary objective\r\nPublic landing page Inviting and aspirational Explain the product and create \r\ncuriosity\r\nPlayer experience Mysterious and reassuring Frame the Chronicle without \r\noverpowering it\r\nCaptain's Console Focused and operational Support fast, reliable live control\r\nVoyagewright Studio Clear and encouraging Help creators build valid, \r\n\r\n===== PAGE 4 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.04\r\nContext Tone Primary objective\r\npublishable Chronicles\r\nErrors and recovery Calm and direct Explain impact, safety, and next \r\naction\r\nFinale or reflection Minimal and spacious Let the Chronicle earn the emotion\r\nCritical distinction\r\nA confirmation dialog for deleting a Chronicle should not recite poetry about the cruel tide of \r\nimpermanence. Tone serves the user's need before it serves atmosphere.\r\n3. Six Governing Principles\r\nThese rules apply across the entire product.\r\n3.1 Clear before clever\r\nThe user must never have to decode the interface itself. Atmosphere may accompany meaning, but it may not \r\nreplace meaning.\r\nGOOD Awaiting the Captain. Your progress is saved. The \r\nnext chapter will appear when it is released.\r\nAVOID The tide has not yet turned. Perhaps the keeper of \r\nthe voyage still ponders what lies beyond.\r\n3.2 Suggest wonder instead of announcing it\r\nVoyagewright should rarely call something epic, magical, incredible, immersive, or unforgettable. The \r\nexperience should earn those reactions.\r\nGOOD A new mark has appeared on the chart.\r\nAVOID An incredible magical discovery has been revealed!\r\n3.3 Nautical texture, not pirate cosplay\r\nUse nautical vocabulary when it improves identity and comprehension. Avoid fake pirate dialect in the platform \r\nvoice.\r\nGOOD Return to your Chronicle Library.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 5 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.05\r\nAVOID Sail back to yer trove of tales, matey!\r\n3.4 Emotion is observed, not commanded\r\nPresent evidence and trust the player. Do not prescribe the correct emotional response.\r\nGOOD The second handwriting ends here. The remaining \r\npages are his alone.\r\nAVOID This heartbreaking discovery fills you with sadness.\r\n3.5 The player is capable and must never be shamed\r\nHints, wrong answers, recovery, and verification failures should preserve dignity and support progress.\r\nGOOD That answer does not fit the markings yet. Review \r\nthe eastern symbol and try again.\r\nAVOID Incorrect. Try harder.\r\n3.6 Humor creates breathing room\r\nHumor should be dry, restrained, and situational. It belongs in harmless moments, not in security, privacy, \r\nbilling, or data-loss states.\r\nGOOD No Chronicles found. Even legends have to be \r\nwritten first.\r\nAVOID Your database has sailed into the abyss, taking all \r\nmortal hope with it.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 6 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.06\r\n4. The Six Voice Layers\r\nVoyagewright is a platform with multiple speakers, not one omnipresent narrator.\r\n4.1 The Voyagewright brand voice\r\nThe most aspirational form of the voice. It may be poetic, but it must still explain what the product does.\r\nWhere it appears: Product website, Marketing pages, Trailer copy, Onboarding, Product descriptions, Public \r\nempty states.\r\n Create stories worth entering.\r\n Build a Chronicle. Gather your crew. Turn an ordinary game night into a voyage they will remember.\r\n Your games already provide the world. Voyagewright gives that world a story made for your crew.\r\n4.2 The platform voice\r\nNeutral, concise, lightly atmospheric, and never pretending to be a story character.\r\nWhere it appears: Navigation, Authentication, Invitations, Loading states, Settings, System notifications.\r\n Invitation accepted.\r\n Your voyage is ready.\r\n Progress saved.\r\n The next chapter has not been released.\r\n No active voyages.\r\n4.3 The Chronicle narrator\r\nThis voice belongs to the Chronicle. It may be a Captain, scientist, ghost, archivist, dispatcher, unreliable \r\nnarrator, or no narrator at all.\r\nWhere it appears: Narration, Journal entries, Letters, Character dialogue, Authored documents, Chronicle-\r\nspecific prompts.\r\n Every Chronicle owns its own narrator and character voices.\r\n Voyagewright does not force nautical narration into non-nautical stories.\r\n Platform errors never masquerade as Chronicle prose.\r\n4.4 The Player experience voice\r\nA bridge between platform clarity and Chronicle atmosphere. Functional truth comes first; atmospheric meaning \r\nfollows.\r\nWhere it appears: Chapter releases, Artifact reveals, Progress framing, Waypoint updates, Collection states.\r\n Chapter III released. A new entry has opened in the journal.\r\n Artifact recovered. The sea-glass lens has been added to your collection.\r\n A new Echo was found. Some places keep more than treasure.\r\n4.5 The Captain interface voice\r\nOperational, fast, and unambiguous. The Captain may be navigating, speaking, coordinating, and avoiding \r\ncannon fire at the same time.\r\nWhere it appears: Session controls, Approvals, Hints, Player status, Chapter releases, Live recovery.\r\n Release Chapter III\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 7 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.07\r\n Awaiting player confirmation\r\n Approve discovery\r\n Send Hint 2\r\n Pause voyage\r\n The player is offline. This action will be delivered when they reconnect.\r\n4.6 The Studio voice\r\nCreator-to-creator language: encouraging, structurally intelligent, and comfortable using ordinary design terms.\r\nWhere it appears: Authoring tools, Validation, Publishing, Versioning, Asset management, Preview.\r\n Create Chronicle\r\n Add chapter\r\n Add passage\r\n Preview from here\r\n Chapter IV cannot be published because it has no completion path.\r\n Publishing creates a permanent version. Existing voyages will continue using their current version.\r\nBoundary rule\r\nThe UI must always know who owns the words. System copy, Captain instructions, Chronicle narration, \r\ncharacter dialogue, and private creator notes are different content classes and must not leak into one \r\nanother.\r\n5. Error and Recovery Language\r\nErrors should be calm, specific, honest, and actionable.\r\nEvery recovery message should answer four questions:\r\n1. What happened?\r\n2. Was anything lost?\r\n3. What is the system doing now?\r\n4. What can the user do next?\r\nSituation Heading Supporting copy\r\nConnection loss Connection lost Your progress is safe. \r\nVoyagewright is trying to \r\nreconnect.\r\nFailed reconnect Unable to reconnect Your progress is saved through \r\nChapter II. Check your \r\nconnection, then try again.\r\nExpired invitation This invitation has expired Ask the Captain to create a new \r\ninvitation.\r\nPublishing problem The Chronicle was not published Chapter III contains a passage \r\nwith no valid destination. Your \r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n===== PAGE 8 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.08\r\nSituation Heading Supporting copy\r\ndraft has been preserved.\r\nUnsupported content This Chronicle requires a newer \r\nversion of Voyagewright\r\nUpdate the application before \r\ncontinuing. Your voyage has not \r\nadvanced.\r\nVerification failure The object could not be confirmed Adjust the view so the full marker \r\nis visible, then try again.\r\nGOOD The Chronicle could not be opened. Your progress \r\nhas not changed. Try again, or return to the Library.\r\nAVOID Something went wrong.\r\n6. Canonical Vocabulary\r\nA shared vocabulary makes the product feel coherent and keeps implementation terminology from \r\nleaking into the interface.\r\nConcept Canonical term Usage note\r\nAuthored playable experience Chronicle The central content object created \r\nin Studio.\r\nActive playthrough Voyage A live or resumable instance of a \r\nChronicle.\r\nPerson running the experience Captain The operator guiding a voyage.\r\nParticipating players Crew The people taking part in the \r\nvoyage.\r\nMajor story division Chapter A large authored section of a \r\nChronicle.\r\nIndividual authored unit Passage A narration, objective, riddle, \r\nchoice, reveal, checkpoint, or \r\ntransition.\r\nLocation or progression point Waypoint A destination, checkpoint, or \r\nmeaningful position.\r\nOptional discovered memory Echo A side discovery that deepens \r\ncontext without blocking progress.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 9 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.09\r\nConcept Canonical term Usage note\r\nSignificant collected object Artifact A discovered object with narrative \r\nmeaning.\r\nCompleted-session record Voyage Record The durable history of a completed \r\nor ended voyage.\r\nCreator application Voyagewright Studio The authoring and publishing \r\ninterface.\r\nPlayer application Voyagewright Player The player-facing experience.\r\nSession-control application Captain's Console The live operating interface.\r\nTest playthrough Preview Voyage A controlled test run of a draft or \r\npublished version.\r\nCompleted or retired content \r\nstorage\r\nArchive A clear ordinary term that requires \r\nno decorative synonym.\r\nWhy Passage works\r\nInternally, the system may use a type such as StoryBlock. Creators should see Passage. A Passage can \r\ncontain narration, a riddle, an objective, a confirmation, an artifact reveal, an image transformation, a choice, a \r\nCaptain checkpoint, or a chapter ending. It feels authored and narrative without pretending every unit is a full \r\nscene.\r\nDo not theme words that are already clear\r\nSave, Cancel, Delete, Email, Password, Privacy, Version history, and Validation errors are already \r\ncompetent words. Renaming a password a 'secret Captain's cipher' would create charm for seven seconds \r\nand accessibility debt for years.\r\n7. Microcopy Transformations\r\nReplace generic or inherited language with copy that is clear, consistent, and distinctly Voyagewright.\r\nGeneric or inherited Voyagewright\r\nStart Tall Tale Begin Chronicle\r\nActive game session Active Voyage\r\nCreate new story Create Chronicle\r\nGame master dashboard Captain's Console\r\nContinue Continue Voyage\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 10 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.010\r\nGeneric or inherited Voyagewright\r\nSubmit Confirm Answer\r\nYou unlocked an item! Artifact recovered\r\nWaiting for admin Awaiting the Captain\r\nNo data Nothing has been recorded yet\r\nReplay animation Replay Presentation\r\nGo back Return to Library\r\nEnd session End Voyage\r\nDuplicate campaign Create a new voyage from this Chronicle\r\nError loading story This Chronicle could not be opened\r\nTesting mode Preview Voyage\r\nDraft game Draft Chronicle\r\nMicrocopy pattern\r\nFor important Player events, use functional truth first and atmospheric meaning second.\r\nExample: 'Chapter III released' followed by 'A new entry has opened in the journal.'\r\n8. Sentence-Level Standards\r\nSmall language choices determine whether the product feels fluent or assembled.\r\nUse sentence case\r\nGOOD Create Chronicle\r\nAVOID CREATE CHRONICLE NOW\r\nPrefer active voice\r\nGOOD The Captain released Chapter II.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 11 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.011\r\nAVOID Chapter II has been released by the Captain.\r\nKeep button labels short\r\nGOOD Invite Crew\r\nAVOID Send an Invitation to Members of Your Crew\r\nUse contractions naturally\r\nGOOD We couldn't reconnect.\r\nAVOID We could not reconnect.\r\nUse exclamation marks rarely\r\nGOOD Settings saved.\r\nAVOID Settings saved!!!\r\nUse ellipses only for ongoing actions\r\nGOOD Reconnecting...\r\nAVOID A secret has been revealed...\r\nAddress the user directly when useful\r\nGOOD Your progress is safe.\r\nAVOID The user's progress has been persisted.\r\nDo not narrate internal emotion\r\nGOOD The ribbon is faded. The knot is still holding.\r\nAVOID You feel a wave of sorrow.\r\nUse concrete verbs\r\nGOOD Release Chapter III\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 12 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.012\r\nAVOID Proceed with chapter-related action\r\nName the object\r\nGOOD This invitation has expired.\r\nAVOID It is no longer valid.\r\nPunctuation and capitalization\r\n Use sentence case for buttons, labels, dialogs, and settings.\r\n Use title case only for proper product names, Chronicle titles, chapter titles, and named interfaces.\r\n Avoid decorative punctuation that competes with the content.\r\n Use an ellipsis only when the operation is genuinely continuing.\r\n Use exclamation marks for rare moments of real delight, not routine writes to a database.\r\n9. Language Across the Emotional Arc\r\nLanguage should change with the Chronicle's pacing without changing Voyagewright's underlying \r\nintegrity.\r\nStage Tone Language behavior Example\r\nEarly Chronicle Energetic, curious, lightly \r\nplayful\r\nShorter sentences, \r\nclearer direction, more \r\nroom for restrained \r\nhumor.\r\nThe journal opens \r\nreluctantly. Its clasp \r\nseems to have opinions.\r\nMiddle movement Observant and widening More space, fewer jokes, \r\nincreasing attention to \r\nearlier details and altered \r\nmeaning.\r\nThis route was never \r\nabandoned. It was left \r\nunfinished for someone \r\nelse.\r\nReflection chapters Quiet and concrete Less interface chatter, \r\nsimpler language, \r\nattention to ordinary \r\nobjects and physical \r\nevidence.\r\nThe ribbon is faded. The \r\nknot is still holding.\r\nFinale Minimal and spacious No marketing language, \r\nno emotional instructions, \r\nno unnecessary \r\nexplanation, and clear \r\nmoral agency.\r\nThe Compass has \r\nchosen its bearing. What \r\nremains is yours to \r\ndecide.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 13 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.013\r\nNarrative rule\r\nReveal the thing. Do not explain the correct emotional response to the thing.\r\n10. Speaker Ownership\r\nEvery meaningful line should have a known speaker and delivery context.\r\nSpeaker ownership prevents system errors from sounding like Chronicle narration, private Captain notes from \r\nappearing in Player content, and one Chronicle's voice from leaking into another.\r\nRecommended content ownership fields\r\n speakerType\r\n speakerId\r\n voiceProfile\r\n deliveryContext\r\n audience\r\n tone\r\n visibility\r\n localizationKey\r\n contentVersion\r\nRecommended speaker types\r\nSpeaker type Purpose\r\nSYSTEM Neutral platform information, status, errors, and \r\nrecovery.\r\nNARRATOR Chronicle-authored narrative voice.\r\nCHARACTER Named or implied character dialogue.\r\nCAPTAIN_OPERATOR Instructions or messages sent by the live Captain.\r\nCREATOR Creator-authored notes and guidance.\r\nPLAYER Player-generated responses or submissions.\r\nUNATTRIBUTED_DOCUMENT Letters, journals, plaques, signs, or found text \r\nwithout a speaking character.\r\nSystem behavior\r\nThe interface should always be able to answer: Who is speaking? To whom? In what context? Is this line \r\nplatform-owned, Chronicle-owned, or user-generated?\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 14 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.014\r\n11. Nautical Intensity\r\nThe nautical metaphor should create identity without limiting the platform's future.\r\nSurface Approximate nautical intensity Guidance\r\nBrand and marketing 40% Voyage, chart, course, crew, \r\nlantern, Chronicle.\r\nNavigation and product \r\nterminology\r\n25% Library, Voyage, Captain, Studio, \r\nArchive.\r\nOrdinary app controls 10% Prefer Save, Cancel, Delete, \r\nPassword, Privacy.\r\nCaptain's Console 15% Operational language with light \r\nnautical framing.\r\nVoyagewright Studio 5-10% Use ordinary creator and \r\npublishing terminology.\r\nErrors, security, privacy, billing 0% Direct, literal, and unthemed.\r\nChronicle content Author-defined The Chronicle's genre and \r\nnarrator control the voice.\r\nThe metaphor of a voyage can support a pirate mystery, science-fiction expedition, fantasy pilgrimage, \r\ndetective investigation, romantic scavenger hunt, historical walking tour, horror experience, or cooperative \r\npuzzle campaign. Fake pirate speech cannot.\r\n12. Accessibility and Localization\r\nFluent language must remain understandable, translatable, and usable by assistive technology.\r\nAccessibility language requirements\r\n Do not use atmosphere as the only carrier of status or instruction.\r\n Pair poetic supporting copy with a literal heading when the action matters.\r\n Do not encode success, warning, or error only through color or decorative iconography.\r\n Use button labels that remain meaningful outside visual context.\r\n Avoid idioms in security, privacy, verification, and recovery messages.\r\n Preserve the same semantic order in full, gentle, and reduced-motion modes.\r\n Do not shame the user for requesting hints, using accessibility settings, or failing verification.\r\n Keep status announcements concise enough for screen readers.\r\nLocalization requirements\r\n Store platform strings separately from Chronicle-authored prose.\r\n Use stable localization keys rather than using English copy as identifiers.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.8 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n===== PAGE 15 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.015\r\n Avoid string concatenation that produces broken grammar in other languages.\r\n Allow button widths and dialog layouts to expand for longer translations.\r\n Preserve speaker identity and tone metadata through translation workflows.\r\n Do not translate Chronicle names, character names, or invented terms without creator intent.\r\n Provide translators with context, audience, and speaker information.\r\nPlain-language override\r\nWhen safety, privacy, data loss, payment, or account access is involved, literal language overrides \r\natmosphere.\r\n13. Copy Governance\r\nVoice quality survives only when it becomes part of the workflow.\r\nRequired governance artifacts\r\n A canonical terminology registry shared by product, design, engineering, documentation, and marketing.\r\n A screen-by-screen copy inventory with ownership and status.\r\n Reusable patterns for errors, confirmations, loading states, empty states, hints, and invitations.\r\n A Chronicle voice profile schema for narrator and character guidance.\r\n A content review checklist included in pull requests and release reviews.\r\n Automated detection for banned inherited terms such as Tall Tale in platform-owned copy.\r\n Versioned migration notes when canonical terminology changes.\r\nRecommended review sequence\r\n5. Confirm the speaker and audience.\r\n6. Write the functional truth in plain language.\r\n7. Add only the atmosphere that improves the moment.\r\n8. Check the canonical glossary.\r\n9. Check the action label independently from surrounding visual context.\r\n10. Verify error and recovery copy explains impact and next action.\r\n11. Test reduced-motion, mobile, and screen-reader presentation.\r\n12. Review the line inside the complete user flow, not in isolation.\r\nImplementation rule\r\nDo not polish language piecemeal while terminology remains unsettled. Establish the glossary and voice \r\nlayers first, then audit the product systematically.\r\n14. Definitive Voice Statement\r\nThe governing statement for all future copy decisions.\r\nVoyagewright speaks with measured wonder. It is warm, observant, precise, \r\nand quietly imaginative. It uses nautical language as texture rather than \r\n\r\n===== PAGE 16 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.016\r\ncostume. It gives clear direction, trusts the player's intelligence, protects their \r\ndignity, and never explains an emotion that the experience can earn. The \r\nplatform speaks as a guide; the Chronicle speaks through its own author and \r\ncharacters.\r\nThe three-line test\r\nClear first.\r\nHuman always.\r\nWonder earned.\r\nDecision rule\r\nWhen two versions are equally clear, choose the more human one. When one version is clearer, choose \r\nclarity. When neither is clear, stop decorating the sentence and fix the sentence.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 17 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.017\r\nAppendix A. Sample Copy Library\r\nOnboarding\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nWelcome to Voyagewright Create a Chronicle, gather your crew, and turn a \r\nfamiliar world into a story made for them.\r\nChoose your role Join as a Player, guide the voyage as Captain, or \r\ncreate in Studio.\r\nNo account yet? Create one to save your Chronicles, voyages, and \r\ninvitations.\r\nInvitations\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nYou've been invited A Captain has invited you to join a voyage.\r\nInvitation accepted Your place in the crew is confirmed.\r\nInvitation expired Ask the Captain to create a new invitation.\r\nCrew member joined Kato has joined the voyage.\r\nPlayer progression\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nChapter released A new entry has opened in the journal.\r\nWaypoint revealed A new bearing has been marked on the chart.\r\nArtifact recovered The object has been added to your collection.\r\nEcho discovered This memory is optional, but it may change what the \r\njourney means.\r\nAwaiting the Captain Your progress is saved. The voyage will continue \r\nwhen the next passage is released.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 18 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.018\r\nCaptain controls\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nRelease chapter Make the next chapter available to the crew.\r\nApprove discovery Confirm the player's submission and advance the \r\nvoyage.\r\nSend hint Reveal the selected hint to the player.\r\nPause voyage Players can keep reading, but progression controls \r\nwill be suspended.\r\nEnd voyage The current voyage will close. Its record and \r\nprogress will be preserved.\r\nStudio\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nCreate Chronicle Start with a title, premise, and first chapter.\r\nAdd passage Choose narration, objective, riddle, choice, reveal, or \r\nCaptain checkpoint.\r\nPreview from here Run the Chronicle from this passage without \r\nchanging the draft.\r\nDraft saved Your latest changes are preserved.\r\nReady to publish Publishing creates a permanent version for new \r\nvoyages.\r\nCannot publish yet Two passages have no valid destination.\r\nEmpty states\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nNo Chronicles yet Every voyage starts with a first page.\r\nNo active voyages Join an invitation or begin one from your Chronicle \r\nLibrary.\r\nNo artifacts recovered Recovered artifacts will appear here.\r\nNo crew invited Invite players when the Chronicle is ready.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 19 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.019\r\nRecovery\r\nHeading or action Supporting copy\r\nConnection lost Your progress is safe. Voyagewright is trying to \r\nreconnect.\r\nUnable to reconnect Your progress is preserved. Check your connection, \r\nthen try again.\r\nUpload incomplete The file was not saved. Choose it again after \r\nchecking your connection.\r\nVoyage paused The Captain has paused progression. Your current \r\nchapter remains available.\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 20 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.020\r\nAppendix B. Copy Review Checklist\r\nSpeaker\r\n ☐ Is the speaker known?\r\n ☐ Is the audience known?\r\n ☐ Is this platform copy, Chronicle content, Captain communication, creator guidance, or user-\r\ngenerated text?\r\n ☐ Could this line leak private or unpublished information?\r\nClarity\r\n ☐ Does the user know what happened?\r\n ☐ Does the user know what to do next?\r\n ☐ Does the heading make sense without decorative supporting copy?\r\n ☐ Would the label remain clear outside its current visual layout?\r\nVoice\r\n ☐ Does the line feel warm, precise, and restrained?\r\n ☐ Is nautical language serving comprehension or merely decorating it?\r\n ☐ Does the line trust the user's intelligence?\r\n ☐ Does it avoid prescribing emotion?\r\nTone\r\n ☐ Is the tone appropriate for the seriousness of the moment?\r\n ☐ Has humor been removed from security, privacy, billing, or data-loss states?\r\n ☐ Is a live Captain action direct enough to use under pressure?\r\n ☐ Is the finale quieter rather than louder?\r\nTerminology\r\n ☐ Does the line use the canonical glossary?\r\n ☐ Has inherited Tall Tale language been removed from platform-owned copy?\r\n ☐ Are Save, Cancel, Delete, Password, Privacy, and Version history left literal where \r\nappropriate?\r\n ☐ Are Chronicle, Voyage, Chapter, Passage, Waypoint, Echo, and Artifact used consistently?\r\nAccessibility and localization\r\n ☐ Can a screen reader understand the action and state?\r\n ☐ Is meaning conveyed without relying only on color, animation, or iconography?\r\n ☐ Will the string survive translation without concatenation problems?\r\n ☐ Does the translation context include speaker, audience, and delivery context?\r\nRecovery\r\n ☐ Does the message explain whether progress or data was lost?\r\n ☐ Does it state what the system is doing now?\r\n ☐ Does it give a concrete next step?\r\n ☐ Does it preserve the user's dignity?\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 21 =====\r\n\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT  |  LANGUAGE DESIGN FOUNDATION\r\nAbsolute Relative Systems  |  Foundation v1.021\r\nFinal approval\r\nA line is ready when it is clear without context, fluent within context, owned by the correct speaker, \r\nconsistent with the glossary, and appropriate for accessibility, localization, and the emotional moment.\r\nVOYAGEWRIGHT\r\nStories made to be played.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

expired\r\n\r\nAsk the Captain to create a new invitation\r\n\r\nClearly distinguish:\r\n\r\ninvitation to join a Voyage;\r\ninvitation to collaborate on a Chronicle;\r\ninvitation to act as surface:\r\n\r\nPlayer home;\r\nChronicle Library;\r\nactive Voyages;\r\ninvitation entry;\r\nrole selection;\r\nVoyage entry;\r\njournal;\r\nChapters;\r\nPassages;\r\nWaypoints;\r\nmap;\r\nArtifact collection;\r\nEchoes;\r\nmessages;\r\nhint flow;\r\nverification;\r\nchapter releases;\r\nreconnect;\r\nreplay;\r\ncompleted Voyage;\r\nreturn visits;\r\nmobile navigation;\r\naccessibility mode;\r\nreduced motion;\r\nloading states;\r\nempty states.\r\n\r\nApply functional-truth-first structure.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nChapter released\r\nA new entry has opened in the journal.\r\n\r\nAwaiting the Captain\r\nYour progress is saved.\r\n\r\nArtifact recovered\r\nAdded to your collection.\r\n\r\nNo active Voyages\r\nJoin an invitation or begin one from your Chronicle Library.\r\n\r\nContinue Voyage\r\n\r\nReturn to Chronicle Library\r\n\r\nReplay presentation\r\n\r\nDo not use Replay animation in production-facing copy.\r\n\r\nThe user is replaying a presentation or ceremony, not inspecting an implementation detail.\r\n\r\n10.6 Captainâ€™s Console\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\nVoyage creation;\r\nparticipant management;\r\nreadiness;\r\nChapter release;\r\nPassage advancement;\r\napprovals;\r\ndenials;\r\nhints;\r\nplayer status;\r\nconnectivity;\r\npause;\r\nresume;\r\nend;\r\nrollback;\r\noverride;\r\ndebug visibility;\r\nverification;\r\npending actions;\r\noffline delivery;\r\nmulti-player synchronization;\r\nactivity log;\r\nwarnings;\r\ndestructive confirmation.\r\n\r\nEvery control must be operationally clear.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\nBegin Voyage\r\n\r\nRelease Chapter\r\n\r\nAdvance to next Passage\r\n\r\nApprove discovery\r\n\r\nRequest another image\r\n\r\nSend Hint 2\r\n\r\nPause Voyage\r\n\r\nResume Voyage\r\n\r\nEnd Voyage\r\n\r\nRemove player\r\n\r\nThis action advances the entire Crew.\r\n\r\nThis player is offline.\r\nThe action will be delivered when they reconnect.\r\n\r\nDangerous actions must state impact.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\nEnd this Voyage?\r\n\r\nPlayers will no longer be able to advance.\r\nThe Voyage Record and current progress will be preserved.\r\n\r\nIf an override changes authoritative state:\r\n\r\nJump to Chapter IV?\r\n\r\nThis will move the entire Crew forward and may skip unfinished Passages.\r\nThe override will be recorded in the Voyage history.\r\n\r\nNo poetic ambiguity is allowed in Captain controls.\r\n\r\n10.7 Voyagewright Studio\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\ndashboard;\r\nChronicle Library;\r\ncreate;\r\nduplicate;\r\nimport;\r\nexport;\r\neditor;\r\nChapters;\r\nPassages;\r\nconditional paths;\r\nobjectives;\r\nanswers;\r\nhints;\r\nCaptain recovery.\r\n\r\nPreferred terms:\r\n\r\nCreate Chronicle\r\nEdit Chronicle\r\nDuplicate Chronicle\r\nArchive Chronicle\r\nDelete Chronicle\r\nAdd Chapter\r\nAdd Passage\r\nAdd Waypoint\r\nAdd Artifact\r\nAdd Echo\r\nPreview Voyage\r\nValidate Chronicle\r\nPublish Chronicle\r\nVersion history\r\nDraft saved\r\nUnpublished changes\r\n\r\nValidation must be useful.\r\n\r\nBad:\r\n\r\nInvalid mark it as the Chapter ending.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nThis Artifact is missing Player artwork\r\n\r\nAdd the image that should appear after the Artifact is recovered.\r\n\r\nGood:\r\n\r\nTwo answers use history\r\n\r\nClearly distinguish:\r\n\r\nChronicle\r\n\r\nfrom:\r\n\r\nVoyage\r\n\r\nA Chronicle is the authored work.\r\n\r\nA Voyage is a playthrough of that work.\r\n\r\nTherefore:\r\n\r\nChronicle Library\r\n\r\ncontains authored or available Chronicles.\r\n\r\nActive Voyages\r\n\r\ncontains current playthroughs.\r\n\r\nVoyage Polish Requirements\r\n\r\nThis implementation is not complete when terminology is merely correct.\r\n\r\nEvery major surface must be polished.\r\n\r\nReview for:\r\n\r\nrhythm;\r\nduplicate wording;\r\nexcessive headings;\r\ninconsistent button labels;\r\nabrupt tone changes;\r\nunclear status;\r\nempty-state quality;\r\nerror usefulness;\r\naction specificity;\r\nhierarchy;\r\ntitle consistency;\r\nmobile truncation;\r\ndialog balance;\r\npunctuation;\r\ncapitalization;\r\nline length;\r\naccessibility;\r\nlocalization resilience.\r\n\r\nAvoid repetitive constructions such as:\r\n\r\nYour Voyage is ready.\r\nYour Chapter is ready.\r\nYour Artifact is ready.\r\nYour hint is ready.\r\n\r\nUse context-specific language.\r\n\r\nAvoid overusing:\r\n\r\njourney\r\nlegend\r\nmystery\r\nunforgettable\r\nawaits\r\ndiscover\r\nembark\r\n\r\nVoyagewright should sound elegant, not like every sentence was generated by a tourism brochure trapped near a thesaurus.\r\n\r\n18. Tales.\",\r\n  },\r\n] as const;\r\n\r\nfunction deterministicStarStyle(index: number): CSSProperties {\r\n  const seeded = (index * 9301 + 49297) % 233280;\r\n  const phase = seeded / 233280;\r\n  return {\r\n    \"--star-duration\": `${3.6 + phase * 4.8}s`,\r\n    \"--star-delay\": `${-phase * 7.2}s`,\r\n    \"--star-min-opacity\": String(0.34 + (index % 4) * 0.08),\r\n  } as CSSProperties;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction roleObjectIntent(role: (typeof roles)[number][\"id\"], mode: ReturnType<typeof useMotionMode>[\"mode\"]) {\r\n  if (mode === \"reduced\") return {};\r\n  if (role === \"captain\") return { whileHover: { rotate: 2.5 } };\r\n  if (role === \"creator\") return { whileHover: { y: -2, rotate: -1 } };\r\n  return { whileHover: { scale: 1.012 } };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function HarborLanding() {\r\n  const motionMode = useMotionMode();\r\n  return (\r\n    <SceneHost as=\"main\" kind=\"gateway\" className=\"harbor-landing role-gateway\" data-motion-mode={motionMode.mode}>\r\n      <HarborGatewayContent motionMode={motionMode} />\r\n    </SceneHost>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction HarborGatewayContent({ motionMode }: { motionMode: useOptionalSceneHost();\r\n  const { director, snapshot } = useAnimationDirector();\r\n  const { mode, cycle } = motionMode;\r\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState<GatewayStatus | null>(null);\r\n  const [selectedRole, setSelectedRole] = useState<(typeof roles)[number][\"id\"] | null>(null);\r\n  const [rememberedBadges, setRememberedBadges] = useState<ReadonlySet<string>>(new Set());\r\n  const starStyles = useMemo(() => Array.from({ length: 28 }, (_, index) => deterministicStarStyle(index)), []);\r\n\r\n  const bindRootSentinel = useCallback((sentinel: HTMLSpanElement | null) => {\r\n    root.current = sentinel?.parentElement instanceof HTMLElement ? sentinel.parentElement : null;\r\n  }, []);\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindSky, handle: skyHandle } = moonHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(harborTargets.moon);\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindHorizon, handle: horizonHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(harborTargets.horizon);\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindOcean, handle: oceanHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(harborTargets.ocean);\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindFogBack, handle: fogBackHandle } = { bindTarget: bindArrivalCopyEyebrow, handle: arrivalCopyEyebrowHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(\r\n    harborTargets.arrivalCopyEyebrow,\r\n  );\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindArrivalCopyBody, handle: arrivalCopyBodyHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(\r\n    harborTargets.arrivalCopyBody,\r\n  );\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindArrivalActionPrimary, handle: arrivalActionPrimaryHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(\r\n    harborTargets.arrivalActionPrimary,\r\n  );\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindArrivalActionRoles, handle: arrivalActionRolesHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(\r\n    harborTargets.arrivalActionRoles,\r\n  );\r\n  const { bindTarget: bindNauticalBorder, handle: nauticalBorderHandle } = useSceneTargetRegistration(\r\n    harborTargets.nauticalBorder,\r\n  );\r\n  const targetsReady = Boolean(\r\n    skyHandle &&\r\n      starsHandle &&\r\n      moonHandle &&\r\n      horizonHandle &&\r\n      oceanHandle &&\r\n      fogBackHandle &&\r\n      fogFrontHandle &&\r\n      shipHandle &&\r\n      titleHandle &&\r\n      arrivalCopyEyebrowHandle &&\r\n      arrivalCopyBodyHandle &&\r\n      arrivalActionPrimaryHandle &&\r\n      arrivalActionRolesHandle &&\r\n      nauticalBorderHandle,\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  useGSAP(\r\n    () => {\r\n      if (mode === \"reduced\") return;\r\n      gsap.to(\"[data-ambient='lantern']\", {\r\n        rotate: mode === \"full\" ? 1.5 : 0.6,\r\n        duration: 2.8,\r\n        repeat: -1,\r\n        yoyo: true,\r\n        ease: \"sine.inOut\",\r\n        transformOrigin: \"50% 0%\",\r\n      });\r\n    },\r\n    { scope: root, dependencies: [mode], revertOnUpdate: true },\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    let mounted = true;\r\n    void fetch(\"/api/gateway/status\", { cache: \"no-store\" })\r\n      .then((response) => (response.ok ? response.json() : null))\r\n      .then((value: GatewayStatus | null) => {\r\n        if (!mounted) return;\r\n        setStatus(value);\r\n        if (value) {\r\n          const entering = new Set<string>();\r\n          for (const role of roles) {\r\n            if (!value[role.id]?.authenticated) continue;\r\n            const key = platformOneShotKey(\"remembered-session\", role.id, value[role.id]?.continue?.href ?? \"active\");\r\n            if (consumeOneShot(key)) entering.add(role.id);\r\n          }\r\n          setRememberedBadges(entering);\r\n        }\r\n      })\r\n      .catch(() => {\r\n        if (mounted) setStatus(null);\r\n      });\r\n    return () => {\r\n      mounted = false;\r\n    };\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const host = root.current;\r\n    if (!host) return;\r\n    let inView = true;\r\n    let keyboardModality = false;\r\n    const coarsePointer = window.matchMedia?.(\"(pointer: coarse)\").matches ?? false;\r\n    const updateAmbient = () => {\r\n      host.dataset.ambientState = mode === \"reduced\" || document.hidden || !inView ? \"paused\" : \"active\";\r\n    };\r\n    const onVisibility = () => updateAmbient();\r\n    const onKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\r\n      if (event.key === \"Tab\" || event.key.startsWith(\"Arrow\")) {\r\n        keyboardModality = true;\r\n        host.dataset.inputModality = \"keyboard\";\r\n        host.style.removeProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-x\");\r\n        host.style.removeProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-y\");\r\n      }\r\n    };\r\n    const onPointerMove = (event: PointerEvent) => {\r\n      keyboardModality = false;\r\n      host.dataset.inputModality = \"pointer\";\r\n      if (coarsePointer || mode === \"reduced\") return;\r\n      const bounds = host.getBoundingClientRect();\r\n      const x = Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, (event.clientX - bounds.left) / Math.max(bounds.width, 1) - 0.5)) * 2;\r\n      const y = Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, (event.clientY - bounds.top) / Math.max(bounds.height, 1) - 0.5)) * 2;\r\n      host.style.setProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-x\", keyboardModality ? \"0\" : x.toFixed(3));\r\n      host.style.setProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-y\", keyboardModality ? \"0\" : y.toFixed(3));\r\n    };\r\n    const onPointerLeave = () => {\r\n      host.style.setProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-x\", \"0\");\r\n      host.style.setProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-y\", \"0\");\r\n    };\r\n    const observer =\r\n      typeof IntersectionObserver === \"undefined\"\r\n        ? null\r\n        : new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => {\r\n            inView = entry?.isIntersecting ?? true;\r\n            updateAmbient();\r\n          });\r\n    observer?.observe(host);\r\n    document.addEventListener(\"visibilitychange\", onVisibility);\r\n    host.addEventListener(\"keydown\", onKeyDown);\r\n    host.addEventListener(\"pointermove\", onPointerMove);\r\n    host.addEventListener(\"pointerleave\", onPointerLeave);\r\n    updateAmbient();\r\n    return () => {\r\n      observer?.disconnect();\r\n      document.removeEventListener(\"visibilitychange\", onVisibility);\r\n      host.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", onKeyDown);\r\n      host.removeEventListener(\"pointermove\", onPointerMove);\r\n      host.removeEventListener(\"pointerleave\", onPointerLeave);\r\n      host.style.removeProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-x\");\r\n      host.style.removeProperty(\"--harbor-parallax-y\");\r\n    };\r\n  }, [mode]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!root.current || !sceneHost || !targetsReady) return;\r\n    const presentationAbort = new AbortController();\r\n    presentationAbortRef.current = presentationAbort;\r\n    const key = \"tall-tale-role-gateway\";\r\n    const scene =\r\n      automaticSceneRef.current ?? (sessionStorage.getItem(key) === \"seen\" ? \"session-reentry\" : \"first-arrival\");\r\n    automaticSceneRef.current = scene;\r\n    if (scene === \"first-arrival\") sessionStorage.setItem(key, \"seen\");\r\n    void director\r\n      .play(scene, {\r\n        root: root.current,\r\n        queue: false,\r\n        sceneHost,\r\n        hostId: sceneHost.hostId,\r\n        hostKind: sceneHost.kind,\r\n        requestSource: \"automatic\",\r\n        signal: presentationAbort.signal,\r\n      })\r\n      .then(reportNonPresentedReceipt)\r\n      .catch(() => undefined)\r\n function continuation(role: (typeof roles)[number]) {\r\n    const current = status?.[role.id];\r\n    return current?.authenticated && current.continue\r\n      ? current.continue\r\n      : { label:  const presentationAbort = new AbortController();\r\n    presentationAbortRef.current = presentationAbort;\r\n    void director\r\n      .play(\"first-arrival\", {\r\n        root: root.current,\r\n        queue: false,\r\n        sceneHost,\r\n        hostId: sceneHost.hostId,\r\n        hostKind: sceneHost.kind,\r\n        requestSource: \"replay\",\r\n        signal: className=\"harbor-sky\" data-scene-part=\"sky\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <div ref={bindStars} className=\"star-field\" data-scene-part=\"stars\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n          {starStyles.map((style, index) => (\r\n            <i key={index} style={style} />\r\n          ))}\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div ref={bindMoon} className=\"moon-glow\" data-scene-part=\"moon\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n          <i />\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div ref={bindFogBack} className=\"distant-clouds\"
