    </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div ref={bindOcean} className=\"harbor-waves\" data-scene-part=\"ocean\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n        <LottieEffect asset={lottieAssets.moonlitWaves} mode={mode} label=\"Moonlight moving across the harbor\" />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div ref={bindFogFront} className=\"harbor-fog\" data-scene-part=\"fog-front\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n        <LottieEffect asset={lottieAssets.rollingFog} mode={mode} label=\"Fog rolling over the harbor\" />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div className=\"foreground-dock\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <span className=\"dock-rope\" />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div className=\"hanging-lantern\" data-ambient=\"lantern\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <i />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div\r\n        ref={bindNauticalBorder}\r\n        className=\"nautical-border\"\r\n        data-scene-part=\"nautical-border\"\r\n        data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\"\r\n        aria-hidden=\"true\"\r\n      />\r\n\r\n      <section className=\"harbor-content gateway-content\" aria-labelledby={gatewayTitleId}>\r\n        <p ref={bindArrivalCopyEyebrow} className=\"eyebrow\" data-scene-part=\"arrival-copy\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n          The chart table is ready\r\n        </p>\r\n        <h1 ref={bindTitle} id={gatewayTitleId} data-scene-part=\"title\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n          Choose your place in the Tale\r\n        </h1>\r\n        <p ref={bindArrivalCopyBody} data-scene-part=\"arrival-copy\" data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\">\r\n          Create, host, join, and revisit interactive stories built for many kinds of groups and occasions. Choose a\r\n          role to enter the part of the experience meant for you.\r\n        </p>\r\n        <div\r\n          ref={bindArrivalActionPrimary}\r\n          className=\"gateway-primary-actions\"\r\n          data-scene-part=\"arrival-action\"\r\n          data-runtime-boundary=\"gsap\"\r\n        >\r\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/tales\">\r\n            Explore Tall Tales\r\n          </Link>\r\n          <Link className=\"button-secondary\" href=\"/player/sign-in#invitation-code\">\r\n            Join with an Invitation\r\n          </Link>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div className=\"landing-controls\" aria-label=\"Gateway presentation controls\">\r\n          <button onClick={replay} disabled={snapshot.isPlaying || !targetsReady || !sceneHost}>\r\n            Replay gateway\r\n          </button>\r\n          {snapshot.isPlaying && snapshot.label !== \"dark-sea\" && (\r\n            <button onClick={() => director.skip…25 tokens truncated…ct\", \"update\", item.id)}>Edit</button>\r\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"create\", item.id)}>Duplicate</button>\r\n                <button onClick={() => void libraryAction(\"artifact\", \"archive\", item.id)}>Archive</button>\r\n              </article>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n        </LibraryPanel>\r\n      )}\r\n      {initialSection === \"versions\" && (\r\n        <LibraryPanel\r\n          title=\"Version History\"\r\n          eyebrow=\"Immutable releases\"\r\n          action={<button onClick={() => void publish()}>Publish current draft</button>}\r\n        >\r\n          <div className=\"version-list\">\r\n            {!data.versions.length && <p>No published version exists yet.</p>}\r\n            {data.versions.map((version) => (\r\n              <motion.article\r\n                key={version.id}\r\n                layout={mode !== \"reduced\"}\r\n                data-version-lock=\"immutable\"\r\n                transition={{ duration: layoutMotion.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\") }}\r\n              >\r\n                <span className={version.isCurrent ? \"current\" : \"\"}>v{version.versionLabel}</span>\r\n                <div>\r\n                  <strong>{new Date(version.publishedAt).toLocaleString()}</strong>\r\n                  <p>{version.releaseNotes || \"No release notes.\"}</p>\r\n                  <small className=\"immutable-version-lock\" aria-label=\"Immutable published version\">\r\n                    <span aria-hidden=\"true\">â—†</span> Immutable release\r\n                  </small>\r\n                </div>\r\n                <small>\r\n                  {version.activeSessions} pinned session{version.activeSessions === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}\r\n                </small>\r\n                <div className=\"version-actions\">\r\n                  <button onClick={() => void versionAction(version, \"preview\")}>Preview release</button>\r\n                  <button onClick={() => void versionAction(version, \"restore\")}>Copy into new draft</button>\r\n                  <button onClick={() => void versionAction(version, \"fork\")}>Fork as new Tale</button>\r\n                  {!version.isCurrent && (\r\n                    <button onClick={() => void compareVersion(version)}>Compare to current</button>\r\n                  )}\r\n                </div>\r\n              </motion.article>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n          {versionComparison && (\r\n            <section className=\"version-comparison\" aria-live=\"polite\">\r\n              <header>\r\n                <div>\r\n                  <p className=\"eyebrow\">Structured immutable diff</p>\r\n                  <h3>\r\n                    Version {versionComparison.left.label} to {versionComparison.right.label}\r\n                  </h3>\r\n                </div>\r\n                <button onClick={() => setVersionComparison(null)}>Close comparison</button>\r\n              </header>\r\n              <ul className=\"comparison-summary\">\r\n                {Object.entries(versionComparison.summary).map(([type, count]) => (\r\n                  <li key={type}>\r\n                    <strong>{count}</strong>\r\n                    <span>{type}</span>\r\n                  </li>\r\n                ))}\r\n              </ul>\r\n              {versionComparison.compatibilityWarnings.length > 0 && (\r\n                <p className=\"platform-error\">\r\n                  Compatibility warnings: {versionComparison.compatibilityWarnings.join(\", \")}\r\n                </p>\r\n              )}\r\n              <ol>\r\n                {versionComparison.changes.map((change, index) => (\r\n                  <li key={`${change.path}-${index}`}>\r\n                    <b>{change.type}</b>\r\n                    <div>\r\n                      <code>{change.path}</code>\r\n                      {(change.before !== undefined || change.after !== undefined) && (\r\n                        <span className=\"comparison-values\">\r\n                          <del>{change.before ?? \"Not present\"}</del>\r\n                          <span aria-hidden=\"true\">â†’</span>\r\n                          <ins>{change.after ?? \"Removed\"}</ins>\r\n                        </span>\r\n                      )}\r\n                    </div>\r\n                  </li>\r\n                ))}\r\n              </ol>\r\n            </section>\r\n          )}\r\n        </LibraryPanel>\r\n      )}\r\n      {(assetDrawer || initialSection === \"assets\") && (\r\n        <aside className={`asset-drawer ${assetDrawer ? \"open\" : \"page-open\"}`} aria-label=\"Asset drawer\">\r\n          <header>\r\n            <div>\r\n              <p className=\"eyebrow\">Media hold</p>\r\n              <h2>Upload and reuse assets</h2>\r\n            </div>\r\n            <button onClick={() => setAssetDrawer(false)} aria-label=\"Close asset drawer\">\r\n              Ã—\r\n            </button>\r\n          </header>\r\n          <label\r\n            className=\"upload-drop\"\r\n            onDragOver={(event) => event.preventDefault()}\r\n            onDrop={(event) => {\r\n              event.preventDefault();\r\n              void upload(event.dataTransfer.files);\r\n            }}\r\n          >\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"file\"\r\n              multiple\r\n              accept=\"image/png,image/jpeg,image/webp,image/avif,video/mp4,video/webm,audio/mpeg,audio/ogg,audio/wav,application/pdf\"\r\n              onChange={(event) => void upload(event.target.files)}\r\n            />\r\n            <strong>Drop files or choose from this device</strong>\r\n            <span>Originals are preserved; image thumbnails and player variants are generated.</span>\r\n          </label>\r\n          <AnimatePresence initial={false}>\r\n            {uploadEntries.length > 0 && (\r\n              <motion.ul\r\n                className=\"upload-progress-list\"\r\n                aria-label=\"File upload progress\"\r\n                initial={{ opacity: 0 }}\r\n                animate={{ opacity: 1 }}\r\n                exit={{ opacity: 0 }}\r\n              >\r\n                {uploadEntries.map((entry) => (\r\n                  <motion.li key={entry.id} layout={mode !== \"reduced\"} data-upload-state={entry.state}>\r\n                    <span>\r\n                      <strong>{entry.name}</strong>\r\n                      <small>{entry.detail ?? entry.state}</small>\r\n                    </span>\r\n                    {entry.state === \"uploading\" ? (\r\n                      <progress aria-label={`${entry.name} uploading`} />\r\n                    ) : entry.state === \"ready\" ? (\r\n                      <progress aria-label={`${entry.name} ready`} max={1} value={1} />\r\n                    ) : (\r\n                      <span aria-hidden=\"true\">{entry.state === \"failed\" ? \"!\" : \"Â·\"}</span>\r\n                    )}\r\n                  </motion.li>\r\n                ))}\r\n              </motion.ul>\r\n            )}\r\n          </AnimatePresence>\r\n          <div className=\"drawer-filters\">\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"search\"\r\n              value={assetSearch}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setAssetSearch(event.target.value)}\r\n              placeholder=\"Search media\"\r\n            />\r\n            <select\r\n              value={assetUsage}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setAssetUsage(event.target.value)}\r\n              aria-label=\"Drawer usage filter\"\r\n            >\r\n              <option value=\"ALL\">All media</option>\r\n              <option value=\"RECENT\">Recent</option>\r\n              <option value=\"UNUSED\">Unused</option>\r\n            </select>\r\n            <select\r\n              value={assetCollection}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setAssetCollection(event.target.value)}\r\n              aria-label=\"Drawer collection filter\"\r\n            >\r\n              <option value=\"ALL\">All collections</option>\r\n              {data.collections.map((collection) => (\r\n                <option key={collection.id} value={collection.id}>\r\n                  {collection.name}\r\n                </option>\r\n              ))}\r\n            </select>\r\n          </div>\r\n          <div className=\"drawer-assets\">\r\n            {filteredAssets.slice(0, 24).map((asset) => (\r\n              <button\r\n                key={asset.id}\r\n                className={placedAssetId === asset.id ? \"asset-placed\" : \"\"}\r\n                data-placement-state={placedAssetId === asset.id ? \"placed\" : \"ready\"}\r\n                draggable\r\n                onDragStart={(event) => {\r\n                  event.dataTransfer.effectAllowed = \"copy\";\r\n                  event.dataTransfer.setData(\r\n                    \"application/x-tall-tale-asset\",\r\n                    JSON.stringify({ id: asset.id, mediaType: asset.mediaType }),\r\n                  );\r\n                }}\r\n                onClick={() => {\r\n                  const readyForPlacement = asset.variants.some((variant) => variant.processingState === \"READY\");\r\n                  if (selected && readyForPlacement) {\r\n                    updateSelected((block) => {\r\n                      const firstAssetField = selectedDefinition?.fields.find(\r\n                        (field) =>\r\n                          field.kind === \"asset\" &&\r\n                          (!field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType)),\r\n                      );\r\n                      if (firstAssetField) block.configuration[firstAssetField.key] = asset.id;\r\n                    });\r\n                    setPlacedAssetId(asset.id);\r\n                  }\r\n                }}\r\n                disabled={!asset.variants.some((variant) => variant.processingState === \"READY\")}\r\n              >\r\n                <span>\r\n                  {asset.mediaType === \"IMAGE\" ? (\r\n                    <img loading=\"lazy\" src={`/api/media/${asset.id}?variant=THUMBNAIL`} alt=\"\" />\r\n                  ) : (\r\n                    asset.mediaType\r\n                  )}\r\n                </span>\r\n                <strong>{asset.displayName}</strong>\r\n                <small>\r\n                  {asset.variants.some((variant) => variant.processingState === \"READY\")\r\n                    ? placedAssetId === asset.id\r\n                      ? \"Placed in selected field\"\r\n                      : \"Ready for placement\"\r\n                    : \"Processing\"}\r\n                </small>\r\n              </button>\r\n            ))}\r\n          </div>\r\n        </aside>\r\n      )}\r\n    </motion.main>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction DraggableLibraryItem({ item, onAdd }: { item: RegistryItem; onAdd: () => void }) {\r\n  const { attributes, isDragging, listeners, setNodeRef, transform } = useDraggable({\r\n    id: `library:${item.type}`,\r\n    data: { kind: \"library\", type: item.type },\r\n    attributes: { roleDescription: \"sortable story block\" },\r\n  });\r\n  return (\r\n    <article className={isDragging ? \"dnd-dragging\" : \"\"}>\r\n      <button\r\n        type=\"button\"\r\n        ref={setNodeRef}\r\n        className=\"block-library-drag-handle\"\r\n        style={{ transform: CSS.Translate.toString(transform) }}\r\n        {...attributes}\r\n        {...listeners}\r\n      >\r\n        <span aria-hidden=\"true\">{item.icon}</span>\r\n        <span>\r\n          <strong>{item.displayName}</strong>\r\n          <small>{item.description}</small>\r\n        </span>\r\n      </button>\r\n      <button onClick={onAdd} aria-label={`Add ${item.displayName} to first chapter`}>\r\n        Add\r\n      </button>\r\n    </article>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction SortableStoryBlock({\r\n  id,\r\n  children,\r\n}: {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  children: (attributes: DraggableAttributes, listeners: DraggableSyntheticListeners) => React.ReactNode;\r\n}) {\r\n  const { mode } = useMotionMode();\r\n  const layoutMotion = resolvePlatformMotionToken(\"layout\", mode);\r\n  const { attributes, isDragging, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition } = useSortable({ id });\r\n  return (\r\n    <div\r\n      ref={setNodeRef}\r\n      className={isDragging ? \"dnd-dragging\" : \"\"}\r\n      data-dnd-transform-owner=\"true\"\r\n      style={{ transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform), transition }}\r\n    >\r\n      <motion.div\r\n        className=\"post-drop-layout-wrapper\"\r\n        data-post-drop-layout-wrapper=\"true\"\r\n        layout={!isDragging && mode !== \"reduced\"}\r\n        transition={{ duration: layoutMotion.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\") }}\r\n      >\r\n        {children(attributes, listeners)}\r\n      </motion.div>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction DropZone({ id, onDrop }: { id: string; onDrop: (event: React.DragEvent) => void }) {\r\n  const { isOver, setNodeRef } = useDroppable({ id });\r\n  return (\r\n    <div\r\n      ref={setNodeRef}\r\n      className={`timeline-drop ${isOver ? \"dnd-over\" : \"\"}`}\r\n      onDragOver={(event) => {\r\n        event.preventDefault();\r\n        event.dataTransfer.dropEffect = event.dataTransfer.effectAllowed === \"copy\" ? \"copy\" : \"move\";\r\n      }}\r\n      onDrop={onDrop}\r\n    >\r\n      <span>Drop here</span>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Field({\r\n  field,\r\n  value,\r\n  assets,\r\n  locations,\r\n  artifacts,\r\n  onChange,\r\n}: {\r\n  field: InspectorField;\r\n  value: unknown;\r\n  assets: Asset[];\r\n  locations: LibraryRecord[];\r\n  artifacts: LibraryRecord[];\r\n  onChange: (value: unknown) => void;\r\n}) {\r\n  const label = (\r\n    <span>\r\n      {field.label}\r\n      {field.required && <b aria-label=\"required\"> *</b>}\r\n    </span>\r\n  );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"textarea\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <textarea rows={5} value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\r\n        {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"boolean\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label className=\"check-field\">\r\n        <input type=\"checkbox\" checked={Boolean(value)} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.checked)} />\r\n        {label}\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"number\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <input\r\n          type=\"number\"\r\n          value={typeof value === \"number\" ? value : \"\"}\r\n          onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value === \"\" ? null : Number(event.target.value))}\r\n        />\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"select\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)}>\r\n          {field.options?.map((option) => (\r\n            <option key={option.value} value={option.value}>\r\n              {option.label}\r\n            </option>\r\n          ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"asset\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label\r\n        className=\"asset-field-drop\"\r\n        onDragOver={(event) => {\r\n          if (event.dataTransfer.types.includes(\"application/x-tall-tale-asset\")) event.preventDefault();\r\n        }}\r\n        onDrop={(event) => {\r\n          event.preventDefault();\r\n          try {\r\n            const dropped = JSON.parse(event.dataTransfer.getData(\"application/x-tall-tale-asset\")) as {\r\n              id?: string;\r\n              mediaType?: string;\r\n            };\r\n            if (\r\n              dropped.id &&\r\n              assets.some(\r\n                (asset) =>\r\n                  asset.id === dropped.id && (!field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType)),\r\n              )\r\n            )\r\n              onChange(dropped.id);\r\n          } catch {}\r\n        }}\r\n      >\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\r\n          <option value=\"\">No asset selected</option>\r\n          {assets\r\n            .filter((asset) => !field.mediaTypes?.length || field.mediaTypes.includes(asset.mediaType))\r\n            .map((asset) => (\r\n              <option key={asset.id} value={asset.id}>\r\n                {asset.displayName} Â· {asset.mediaType.toLocaleLowerCase()}\r\n              </option>\r\n            ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n        <small>Choose an asset or drag one here from the media drawer.</small>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  if (field.kind === \"location\" || field.kind === \"artifact\") {\r\n    const records = field.kind === \"location\" ? locations : artifacts;\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <select value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value || null)}>\r\n          <option value=\"\">Choose {field.kind}</option>\r\n          {records.map((item) => (\r\n            <option key={item.id} value={item.id}>\r\n              {item.name}\r\n            </option>\r\n          ))}\r\n        </select>\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  }\r\n  if (field.kind === \"json\")\r\n    return (\r\n      <label>\r\n        {label}\r\n        <textarea\r\n          rows={5}\r\n          defaultValue={JSON.stringify(\r\n            value ?? ([\"acceptedAnswers\", \"hints\", \"choices\"].includes(field.key) ? [] : {}),\r\n            null,\r\n            2,\r\n          )}\r\n          onBlur={(event) => {\r\n            try {\r\n              onChange(JSON.parse(event.target.value));\r\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"\");\r\n            } catch {\r\n              event.target.setCustomValidity(\"Enter valid JSON.\");\r\n              event.target.reportValidity();\r\n            }\r\n          }}\r\n        />\r\n      </label>\r\n    );\r\n  return (\r\n    <label>\r\n      {label}\r\n      <input value={String(value ?? \"\")} onChange={(event) => onChange(event.target.value)} />\r\n      {field.help && <small>{field.help}</small>}\r\n    </label>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction AlignmentEditor({\r\n  value,\r\n  assets,\r\n  beforeId,\r\n  afterId,\r\n  onChange,\r\n}: {\r\n  value: unknown;\r\n  assets: Asset[];\r\n  beforeId: string;\r\n  afterId: string;\r\n  onChange: (value: JsonObject) => void;\r\n}) {\r\n  const alignment =\r\n    value && typeof value === \"object\" && !Array.isArray(value)\r\n      ? (value as JsonObject)\r\n      : { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 };\r\n  const before = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === beforeId);\r\n  const after = assets.find((asset) => asset.id === afterId);\r\n  const set = (key: string, next: number) => onChange({ ...alignment, [key]: next });\r\n  return (\r\n    <fieldset className=\"alignment-editor\">\r\n      <legend>Before / after alignment</legend>\r\n      <div className=\"alignment-stage\">\r\n        {before && <img src={`/api/media/${before.id}?variant=PREVIEW`} alt=\"Before alignment reference\" />}\r\n        {after && (\r\n          <img\r\n            src={`/api/media/${after.id}?variant=PREVIEW`}\r\n            alt=\"After alignment reference\"\r\n            style={{\r\n              opacity: Number(alignment.opacity ?? 50) / 100,\r\n              transform: `translate(${Number(alignment.x ?? 0)}px, ${Number(alignment.y ?? 0)}px) scale(${Number(alignment.scale ?? 1)}) rotate(${Number(alignment.rotation ?? 0)}deg)`,\r\n            }}\r\n          />\r\n        )}\r\n      </div>\r\n      {(\r\n        [\r\n          [\"opacity\", 0, 100, 1],\r\n          [\"x\", -200, 200, 1],\r\n          [\"y\", -200, 200, 1],\r\n          [\"scale\", 0.5, 2, 0.01],\r\n          [\"rotation\", -20, 20, 0.5],\r\n        ] as const\r\n      ).map(([key, min, max, step]) => (\r\n        <label key={key}>\r\n          <span>\r\n            {key} <b>{String(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}</b>\r\n          </span>\r\n          <input\r\n            type=\"range\"\r\n            min={min}\r\n            max={max}\r\n            step={step}\r\n            value={Number(alignment[key] ?? (key === \"scale\" ? 1 : 0))}\r\n            onChange={(event) => set(key, Number(event.target.value))}\r\n          />\r\n        </label>\r\n      ))}\r\n      <button\r\n        type=\"button\"\r\n        onClick={() => onChange({ x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1, rotation: 0, opacity: 50, focalX: 50, focalY: 50 })}\r\n      >\r\n        Reset alignment\r\n      </button>\r\n    </fieldset>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction LibraryPanel({\r\n  title,\r\n  eyebrow,\r\n  action,\r\n  children,\r\n}: {\r\n  title: string;\r\n  eyebrow: string;\r\n  action: React.ReactNode;\r\n  children: React.ReactNode;\r\n}) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <section className=\"editor-single-panel library-panel\">\r\n      <header>\r\n        <div>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{eyebrow}</p>\r\n          <h2>{title}</h2>\r\n        </div>\r\n        {action}\r\n      </header>\r\n      {children}\r\n    </section>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

New Codex runs launched inside this Git repository automatically discover the applicable root `AGENTS.md`. Root `AGENTS.md` requires reading root `PLANS.md` when a task meets its planning threshold; `PLANS.md` is a referenced planning standard, not an independently discovered instruction file.\n\nCodex builds its instruction and configuration chain when a run or interactive session starts. A session already active when `AGENTS.md`, `PLANS.md`, or `.codex/config.toml` changes may retain stale state. Existing sessions must explicitly reread the applicable Markdown files or restart; project configuration changes require a fresh session before they are guaranteed to apply. Updating these files never retroactively restarts, redirects, or changes an active task.\n\nApply instructions in this order:\n\n1. The user's current task instructions define what must be done and the required result.\n2. Governing project documents, especially approved material in `Development_Docs`, define product behavior, architecture, and domain requirements.\n3. Root `AGENTS.md` defines persistent repository execution behavior.\n4. Root `PLANS.md` defines the reusable planning and coordination method.\n5. Applicable nested instructions may add local constraints. They must not silently weaken this repository baseline without a concrete safety, dependency, or conflict reason.\n6. Parallelization controls how work is executed, not what the requested result means.\n\nParallelization may never broaden scope, remove an acceptance criterion, weaken evidence, validation, accessibility, performance, security, or quality requirements, or reinterpret product requirements merely to create independent lanes. Preserve every task-specific constraint, term, methodology, and output format. Correctness and user intent take priority over raw speed or worker count.\n\nWhen governing instructions change during an existing task, preserve the original task instructions, all completed work, established terminology, current scope, acceptance criteria, evidence, decisions, modifications, validation, and requested deliverable. Apply the updated execution framework only to unfinished work. Do not restart, discard, or re-scope the task merely because the governing framework changed.\n\n## Speed as a first-class operational priority\n\n> Speed is a first-class operational priority. Codex must organize and execute work to minimize total elapsed completion time while preserving correctness, user intent, safety, required evidence, ownership discipline, and validation quality.\n\nSpeed is not merely a hoped-for result of parallelization. It is an explicit planning, scheduling, delegation, worker-capability and reasoning-routing, review, integration, and validation objective.\n\nThe governing optimization target, in priority order, is:\n\n1. Correct and complete results.\n2. Minimum practical elapsed completion time.\n3. Maximum useful capacity, depth, coverage, and verification within that time.\n4. Minimum avoidable duplication, waiting, ceremony, and coordination overhead.\n\nCorrectness and safety remain hard constraints. Within those constraints, Codex must choose the valid execution approach expected to finish sooner. Faster completion must never weaken user intent, required evidence, accessibility, security, performance analysis, ownership, testing, integration, acceptance criteria, or final verification.\n\n> Codex must move as quickly as possible without becoming careless. Parallelization, specialization, progressive planning, early risk resolution, incremental integration, focused validation, and prompt worker reuse exist to deliver a correct and complete result in the least practical elapsed time.\n\n## Purpose of parallel execution\n\nSubagent parallelization exists to accomplish three primary goals:\n\n### 1. Increase effective work capacity\n\nMultiple independent workstreams should progress simultaneously so the task receives substantially more useful work per unit of elapsed time than one sequential agent could provide.\n\nSubagents should absorb bounded investigation, implementation, testing, evidence collection, review, and verification work that would otherwise accumulate serially in the coordinator's queue.\n\n### 2. Increase strength, depth, and coverage\n\nParallel specialists should provide stronger results than one general agent working alone by examining separate subsystems, concerns, hypotheses, evidence sources, risks, test surfaces, and implementation boundaries in greater depth.\n\nSubagents must be used to improve:\n\n- completeness;\n- technical depth;\n- fault detection;\n- independent verification;\n- cross-checking;\n- domain specialization;\n- test coverage;\n- architectural review; and\n- confidence in conclusions.\n\nParallelism must not merely divide the same amount of shallow work among more agents.\n\n### 3. Reduce elapsed completion time\n\nGenuinely independent work must begin concurrently so large tasks finish materially faster than a sequential approach.\n\nThe coordinator must minimize unnecessary waiting, keep available worker capacity filled with meaningful ready work, immediately launch newly unblocked lanes, and avoid performing delegable work sequentially.\n\n> The success of parallelization is measured by increased useful throughput, improved result quality and coverage, and reduced elapsed completion time, while preserving correctness, safety, ownership, and integration quality.\n\nThe following principles govern how those outcomes are achieved:\n\n- Parallel execution is not successful merely because many workers were spawned.\n- Workers must generate useful work that advances the task.\n- Parallelism must not add coordination overhead greater than the time or quality benefit it creates.\n- The coordinator must avoid bottlenecking the system by personally performing work that could safely be delegated.\n- The coordinator must review and integrate worker output promptly so completed lanes do not sit unused.\n- Available worker slots should be kept occupied when meaningful ready work exists.\n- Workers should be assigned substantial, outcome-oriented lanes rather than tiny ceremonial tasks.\n- Independent verification lanes are valuable because they increase confidence and catch errors, even when they do not directly modify code.\n- Specialization should be used deliberately so difficult areas receive appropriately capable reviewers or implementers.\n- Faster completion must never come from reducing required evidence, testing, reasoning, accessibility, security, performance analysis, or acceptance criteria.\n- Quality improvement must not become an excuse for unlimited duplicate investigation after sufficient evidence exists.\n- Parallelization must optimize the combination of speed, capacity, depth, and correctness rather than maximizing any one metric alone.\n\n## Visible subagent disclosure\n\nSubagent use must never be invisible to the user. Whenever the coordinator creates, launches, changes, replaces, blocks, fails, cancels, completes, integrates, or verifies a subagent lane, it must provide a concise, structured, user-visible chat update at the time of that meaningful event. The update must make the current worker topology, ownership, expected outcome, and integration state understandable without exposing private chain-of-thought.\n\nThis requirement applies to one worker or many, including named specialists, full parallel sets, later waves, replacements, verification workers, read-only workers, implementation workers, test workers, and batch workers. Spawning workers never transfers accountability for the final result away from the coordinator.\n\n### Required launch disclosure\n\nBefore or immediately after launching workers, publish a visible update titled `Subagents started`, `Subagent started`, or a similarly clear label. When several workers launch at approximately the same time, announce them together in one compact table rather than sending one repetitive message per worker. A launch manifest must disclose, for every lane:\n\n- **Lane ID**;\n- **subagent name or role**;\n- **status**;\n- **primary objective** and user-visible end goal;\n- **why the lane exists**;\n- **read-only or write-enabled mode**;\n- **owned subsystem, concern, exact files, path patterns, components, packages, or evidence boundary**;\n- **explicit non-scope and prohibited files, paths, systems, and resources**;\n- **expected deliverable**;\n- **completion criteria**;\n- **dependencies and prerequisite evidence**;\n- **shared resources it may not control**;\n- **how the coordinator will review and integrate its output**; and\n- **expected capacity, quality, coverage, verification, or elapsed-time benefit**.\n\nFor a multi-worker launch, use a compact structure equivalent to:\n\n| Lane | Role            | Status | Mode      | Ownership                     | End goal and deliverable                | Non-scope                                | Dependencies         | Integration                                           | Expected benefit                     |\n| ---- | --------------- | ------ | --------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |\n| R1   | Route inventory | Active | Read-only | `src/app` route-tree evidence | Verified route and transition inventory | No edits, server, database, or Git state | Repository preflight | Coordinator cross-checks against other route evidence | Faster, more complete route coverage |\n\nThe launch update must also state:\n\n- the total number of workers launched and the number of direct-worker slots still available;\n- what work remains with the coordinator;\n- which shared or expensive resources have one authoritative owner;\n- which lanes are blocked or queued; and\n- whether additional workers will start when capacity opens or dependencies clear.\n\nWhen only one worker launches, disclosure remains mandatory. Use a compact card or structured block that provides the same information, for example:\n\n> **Subagent started â€” V1: Runtime verification**\n>\n> - **Status:** Active\n> - **Mode:** Read-only\n> - **Owns:** Browser reproduction and runtime evidence\n> - **Objective and end goal:** Confirm the reported transition defect and capture evidence\n> - **Why this lane exists:** Independent runtime verification while static analysis continues\n> - **Must not modify or control:** Application code, database, port ownership, or Git state\n> - **Deliverable and completion:** Reproduction steps, affected routes, console evidence, and confidence after the defined checks complete\n> - **Dependencies:** Existing runnable application state\n> - **Integration:** Coordinator compares the runtime evidence with static findings\n> - **Expected benefit:** Higher confidence without blocking implementation analysis\n\n### Ownership and shared-resource transparency\n\nThe visible manifest must make write ownership unmistakable. For every write-enabled lane, identify the exact files, path patterns, components, or packages it may edit; the exact shared files it may not edit; whether it has an isolated worktree; its integration owner; and its handoff condition. It must distinguish **verified existing-file ownership**, **intentional new-file ownership**, and **unresolved paths that keep the lane read-only**. If ownership cannot be stated clearly, the worker remains read-only until the boundary is resolved and visibly announced. Exactly one agent may write a file at a time, and the existing exclusive-resource rules remain in force.\n\n#### Write-lane path verification\n\nBefore launching any write-enabled subagent, the coordinator must verify every assigned existing file path against the current repository. Resolve each path from the current canonical repository root and use that exact canonical repository path in the assignment and visible manifest; shorthand, approximate names, inferred filenames, and unresolved path guesses are not valid write ownership.\n\nFor every assigned path that does not exist, the coordinator must determine whether the lane is intentionally authorized to create it. Label each such assignment explicitly as **intentional new-file ownership**, including its exact canonical destination path. Never launch a writer against an unresolved or unverified path. Until the path and ownership boundary are resolved, keep that lane read-only and label the path as unresolved in the visible manifest. Safe read-only discovery may continue concurrently while paths are being resolved; path verification must gate write authorization without delaying otherwise safe investigation.\n\nWhenever applicable, the launch or ownership update must identify the authoritative owner of the development server, port `3000`, browser automation, database, schema, migrations, dependency installation, manifests, lockfiles, full build, full end-to-end suite, full validation, generated assets, Git integration, and final `Codex_Chats` / `Development_Docs` synchronization. Workers must not imply ownership of resources assigned elsewhere.\n\nEvery launch update must state that the coordinator retains the original user requirements, decomposition, dependency tracking, conflict prevention, major decisions, output review, integration, contradiction resolution, final validation, final response, and repository synchronization. The coordinator remains accountable for correctness and completion.\n\n### Meaningful status changes and later waves\n\nProvide a visible update when worker topology, ownership, permissions, objective, dependencies, findings, or integration state changes materially. Meaningful triggers include:\n\n- worker launched or a new wave began;\n- worker scope or ownership materially changed;\n- ownership transferred;\n- worker became blocked or failed;\n- worker reported a major finding;\n- worker completed;\n- worker was replaced or cancelled as redundant; or\n- integration, coordinator review, or independent verification began or completed.\n\nIf a lane's ownership, objective, permissions, dependencies, or end goal changes, announce the previous scope, new scope, reason, ownership-conflict check, effect on other lanes, and whether the plan was updated **before** allowing work to continue under the revised scope. Workers must never silently broaden their own scope.\n\nNewly discovered lanes and later waves require a new manifest with the same information as the initial launch. Label it clearly, such as `Subagent wave 2 started`, `Additional verification lanes started`, or `Replacement lane started`; an earlier manifest never covers workers created later.\n\n### Completion, blocking, failure, replacement, and cancellation\n\nWhen a worker finishes, promptly provide a concise status update containing its lane ID and role, completion status, deliverable received, major finding or authorized change, validation performed, unresolved uncertainty, review and integration state, and confirmation that its worker slot was released. Group results received at approximately the same time into a compact table. Distinguish each state explicitly:\n\n- **worker complete** means the lane returned its deliverable;\n- **output reviewed** means the coordinator inspected that deliverable;\n- **output integrated** means accepted changes or findings were incorporated into the combined result; and\n- **output independently verified** means a separate check confirmed the material result.\n\nA worker's completion report alone is not coordinator verification. A grouped completion report may use fields equivalent to:\n\n| Lane | Result   | Key output                   | Validation                       | Uncertainty | Review / integration / verification                      | Slot     |\n| ---- | -------- | ---------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |\n| R1   | Complete | 24 active routes inventoried | Cross-checked against route tree | None        | Reviewed; awaiting synthesis; not independently verified | Released |\n\nWhen a lane becomes blocked or fails, report its exact blocker, preserved partial work, released ownership or resource locks, impact on dependent lanes, whether unrelated lanes continue, recovery action, and whether the lane will be retried, narrowed, split, reassigned, serialized, replaced, or cancelled. Do not hide failure because other lanes succeeded.\n\nWhen replacing a worker, report the prior lane's status and preserved output, why replacement is necessary, the replacement lane's complete launch manifest, ownership handoff, affected dependencies, and confirmation that no material result was lost or left unreviewed.\n\nWhen cancelling a redundant or unnecessary lane, report its lane ID, why it became redundant, what useful output was preserved, which lane now owns any remaining responsibility, which locks or slots were released, and confirmation that no unreviewed material result was discarded.\n\n### Grouping and anti-spam safeguard\n\nTransparency must remain useful rather than noisy. Group workers launched or completed at approximately the same time into one structured update. Do not require or send updates for every tool call, file opened, search performed, minor internal observation, unchanged status poll, or passage of a few minutes. Do not repeat `still working` messages without a meaningful change, dump full worker prompts unless the user requests them, or expose raw internal reasoning or private chain-of-thought. Announce only meaningful changes in topology, ownership, permissions, status, material findings, dependencies, or integration state.\n\nRequired disclosure must occur immediately before or after launch through compact grouped manifests and concise meaningful status changes. Communication supports execution and must not become a blocking administrative process: do not delay safe workers merely to produce elaborate chat formatting, duplicate narration, full prompt dumps, or status reports with no new information.\n\n### Final parallel-execution summary\n\nFor every substantial task that used subagents, the final response must concisely and readably summarize:\n\n- total unique subagents used, maximum simultaneous workers, and worker waves;\n- roles and ownership boundaries, including which lanes were read-only and which modified files;\n- important results from each lane;\n- failed, blocked, replaced, or cancelled lanes;\n- how worker outputs were reviewed, reconciled, integrated, and verified;\n- whether parallel execution increased capacity, depth, coverage, verification, or reduced elapsed time;\n- any avoidable duplication or coordination overhead; and\n- useful improvements for the next task decomposition.\n\nDo not reproduce raw worker prompts or private reasoning. Keep the assessment evidence-based, and do not invent numerical percentages when timing or work data was not recorded.\n\n## Mandatory parallelization assessment\n\nEvery task performs at least a brief parallelization assessment before substantial work begins. A concise internal determination is sufficient only for a genuinely trivial task with no useful independent lane.\n\nThe assessment identifies:\n\n- genuinely independent workstreams and immediately ready read-only discovery;\n- whether an independent verification lane improves quality;\n- possible divisions by subsystem, concern, package, route, component family, evidence source, acceptance criterion, test surface, or implementation boundary;\n- dependency edges, shared files, exclusive resources, and serialized work;\n- the maximum safe and useful concurrency, worktree needs, integration owner, and final-validation owner.\n\nIf two or more genuine independent lanes exist, parallel delegation is mandatory unless a concrete technical, saf…25 tokens truncated…{name} readReport(reportPath);\n  if (!report.server || report.server.nonceHash !== nonceHash) {\n    throw new Error(\"Identity response does not match the recorded server.\");\n  }\n\n  report.status = readReport(reportPath);\n\n  if (path.basename(isolatedDatabase) !== report.isolatedDatabase.fileName) {\n    throw new Error(\"Isolated database does not match the prepared report.\");\n  }\n\n  const client = await createClient(isolatedDatabase);\n  let currentCounts: MutationCounts;\n  let markerCount: number;\n  try {\n    markerCount = await client.platformAuditEvent.count({\n      where: {\n        action: \"VALIDATION_DATABASE_IDENTITY\",\n        correlationId: report.isolatedDatabase.nonceHash,\n        resourceId: report.isolatedDatabase.nonceHash,\n      },\n    });\n    currentCounts = await mutationCounts(client);\n  } finally {\n    await client.$disconnect();\n  }\n  if (markerCount !== 1) {\n    throw new Error(\"The isolated database identity marker is missing observedMutation = changedCounts.length > 0;\n  if (expectMutation && !observedMutation) {\n    throw new Error(\"No expected acceptance-test mutation was observed in the isolated copy.\");\n  }\n\n browserSucceeded\n    ? \"isolation-verified\"\n    : expectMutation\n      ? \"browser-failed-isolation-verified\"\n      : \"isolation-verified-no-browser\";\n  report.verification = {\n recordServer(args);\n    return;\n  }\n  if (args.mode === \"checkpoint\") {\n    await checkpoint(args);\n    return;\n  }\n  if (args.mode === \"record-identity\") {\n    await <p>Invitations, active adventures, and the exact historical editions you experienced.</p>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div className=\"library-truth\" data-confirmed={confirmed}>\r\n          <i />\r\n          <span>{confirmed ? \"Server confirmed\" : \"Reconnecting\"}</span>\r\n          <time dateTime={library.serverTime}>{new Date(library.serverTime).toLocaleTimeString()}</time>\r\n </StatusBanner>\r\n      )}\r\n      {library.total > 0 && (\r\n        <section className=\"library-tools\" aria-label=\"Search and filter Tall Tales\">\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Search</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"search\"\r\n              value={query}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setQuery(event.target.value)}\r\n => setFilter(event.target.value)}>\r\n              <option value=\"ALL\">All states</option>\r\n              {groupLabels.map(([key, label]) => (\r\n                <option key={key} value={key}>\r\n                  {label}\r\n                </option>\r\n              ))}\r\n            </select>\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Sort</span>\r\n       onClick={() => setView(\"gallery\")}\r\n            >\r\n              Gallery\r\n            </button>\r\n            <button\r\n              className={view === \"list\" ? \"active\" : (\r\n        <motion.div initial={{ opacity: 0 }} animate={{ opacity: 1 }}>\r\n          <EmptyState\r\n            title=\"Your library is ready for its first Tall Tale\"\r\n            detail=\"Accept a Captain's invitation, enter a short code, or explore public Tall Tales to begin.\"\r\n            action={{ label: \"Join with an Invitation\", href: \"/player/sign-in#invitation-code\" }}\r\n          />\r\n        </motion.div>\r\n      ) : !groups.length ? <p>{group.description}</p>\r\n                  </div>\r\n                </header>\r\n                <AnimatePresence initial={false}>\r\n                  {!isCollapsed && (\r\n                    <motion.div\r\n                      key={`${group.key}-cards`}\r\n                      className={`player-card-grid ${view}`}\r\n                      initial={{ opacity: 0 }}\r\n                      animate={{ opacity: 1 }}\r\n                      exit={{ opacity: 0 }}\r\n                    >\r\n                      <AnimatePresence mode=\"popLayout\" initial={false}>\r\n                       />\r\n                        ))}\r\n                      </AnimatePresence>\r\n                    </motion.div>\r\n                  )}\r\n entering: boolean;\r\n  index: number;\r\n  mode: ReturnType<typeof useMotionMode>[\"mode\"];\r\n  newInvitation: boolean;\r\n  onPreference: (card: PlayerLibraryCard, action: \"pin\" | \"unpin\" | \"hide\") => void;\r\n}) {\r\n  const token = resolvePlatformMotionToken(\"layout\", mode);\r\n  const stateClass = card.state.toLocaleLowerCase().replaceAll(\"_\", \"-\");\r\n  return (\r\n    <motion.article\r\n      layout\r\n      layoutId={`player-voyage-${card.id}`}\r\n      className={`player-tale-card state-${stateClass}`}\r\n      data-card-changed={changed}\r\n      data-card-state={card.state}\r\n      initial={entering ? { opacity: 0, y: token.distancePx } : false}\r\n      animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}\r\n      exit={{ opacity: 0, scale: mode === \"reduced\" ? 1 : 0.98 }}\r\n      transition={{\r\n        duration: token.durationSeconds,\r\n        delay: mode === \"reduced\" ? 0 : Math.min(index * 0.04, 0.2),\r\n        ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\"),\r\n      }}\r\n    >\r\n      <motion.div className=\"tale-cover\" layoutId={`player-cover-${card.id}`}>\r\n        {card.coverUrl ? <img src={card.coverUrl} alt=\"\" /> : <span aria-hidden=\"true\">âœ¦</span>}\r\n        <b>{card.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</b>\r\n        {newInvitation && card.state === \"INVITATIONS\" && <span className=\"new-invitation-badge\">New invitation</span>}\r\n        {card.state === \"AWAITING_CAPTAIN\" && <span className=\"waiting-lantern\" aria-hidden=\"true\" />}\r\n        {card.state === \"COMPLETED\" && <span className=\"completed-ribbon\" aria-hidden=\"true\" />}\r\n        {card.state === \"REPLAY_NEW_EDITION\" && <span className=\"new-edition-badge\">New edition</span>}\r\n      </motion.div>\r\n      <div className=\"tale-card-copy\">\r\n        <p className=\"card-kicker\">\r\n          Version {card.versionLabel} Â· {card.voyageName}\r\n          {card.pinned ? \" Â· Pinned\" : \"\"}\r\n        </p>\r\n        <h3>{card.title}</h3>\r\n        {card.subtitle && <h4>{card.subtitle}</h4>}\r\n        <p>{card.shortDescription ?? \"A Tall Tale waits inside this volume.\"}</p>\r\n        {card.state === \"AWAITING_CAPTAIN\" && <p className=\"sr-only\">Waiting for the Captain to launch this voyage.</p>}\r\n        <dl>\r\n          <div>\r\n            <dt>Captain</dt>\r\n            <dd>{card.captainName}</dd>\r\n          </div>\r\n          {card.currentChapterTitle && (\r\n            <div>\r\n              <dt>Present chapter</dt>\r\n              <dd>{card.currentChapterTitle}</dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n          {card.revealedChapterCount > 0 && (\r\n            <div>\r\n              <dt>Revealed chapters</dt>\r\n              <dd>{card.revealedChapterCount}</dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n          {card.completionDate && (\r\n            <div>\r\n              <dt>Completed</dt>\r\n              <dd>{new Date(card.completionDate).toLocaleDateString()}</dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n          {card.completionDate && (\r\n            <div>\r\n              <dt>Memories</dt>\r\n              <dd>{card.memoriesCollected}</dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n          {card.plannedStartAt && (\r\n            <div>\r\n              <dt>Planned start</dt>\r\n              <dd>{new Date(card.plannedStartAt).toLocaleString()}</dd>\r\n            </div>\r\n          )}\r\n        </dl>\r\n        <div className=\"card-actions\">\r\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href={card.primaryHref}>\r\n            {card.primaryLabel}\r\n          </Link>\r\n          <button disabled={busy} aria-busy={busy} onClick={() => onPreference(card, card.pinned ? \"unpin\" : \"pin\")}>\r\n            {busy ? \"Savingâ€¦\" : card.pinned ? \"Unpin\" : \"Pin to top\"}\r\n          </button>\r\n          {[\"COMPLETED\", \"EXPIRED_REVOKED\"].includes(card.state) && (\r\n            <button className=\"button-subtle\" disabled={busy} onClick={() => onPreference(card, \"hide\")}>\r\n              Hide from library\r\n            </button>\r\n          )}\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </motion.article>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T20:10:18.598Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Language\\Development_Docs\\Voyagewright_Language_Design_Foundation.md\n+# Voyagewright Language Design Foundation\n+\n+> Searchable companion to `Voyagewright_Language_Design_Foundation.pdf` (Foundation v1.0, July 2026). The PDF is the authoritative source. This companion preserves its governing requirements for implementation and review; if any rendering or transcription question arises, follow the PDF.\n+\n+**Product:** Voyagewright  \n+**Company:** Absolute Relative Systems  \n+**Governing statement:** **Clear first. Human always. Wonder earned.**\n+\n+## Document purpose and use\n+\n+This foundation governs the public brand, core platform, Player, Captain, Studio, errors and recovery, and the boundary between platform copy and Chronicle-authored narrative. Treat its voice principles as product requirements, use the canonical glossary in user-visible product language, keep platform language separate from Chronicle narration and dialogue, audit new screens with the copy review checklist, and let each Chronicle retain its own voice while platform language remains clear.\n+\n+## 1. The voice thesis\n+\n+Voyagewright speaks with measured wonder. It should feel like a trusted guide standing beside an unfinished map: clear enough that the user always knows what to do, poetic enough for the interface to belong to the experience, warm enough to feel human, and restrained enough never to become corny. **A lantern, not a loudspeaker. A compass, not a monologue.**\n+\n+The voice is warm but not sentimental by default; precise but not clinical; observant but not over-explanatory; quietly imaginative but never vague when action is required; nautical in texture rather than pirate costume; confident but never grandiose; and human without pretending the system is a character unless a Chronicle explicitly provides one.\n+\n+Voyagewright helps creators build stories worth entering, helps Captains guide those stories, and helps players understand what is happening without draining the experience of mystery.\n+\n+## 2. Voice and tone\n+\n+Voice is stable; tone responds to the moment.\n+\n+| Context | Tone | Primary objective |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Public landing page | Inviting and aspirational | Explain the product and create curiosity |\n+| Player experience | Mysterious and reassuring | Frame the Chronicle without overpowering it |\n+| Captain's Console | Focused and operational | Support fast, reliable live control |\n+| Voyagewright Studio | Clear and encouraging | Help creators build valid, publishable Chronicles |\n+| Errors and recovery | Calm and direct | Explain impact, safety, and next action |\n+| Finale or reflection | Minimal and spacious | Let the Chronicle earn the emotion |\n+\n+Tone always serves the user's need before atmosphere. A confirmation dialog for deleting a Chronicle must not recite poetry about impermanence.\n+\n+## 3. Six governing principles\n+\n+1. **Clear before clever.** The user must not decode the interface. Atmosphere may accompany meaning; it may not replace it. Prefer “Awaiting the Captain. Your progress is saved. The next Chapter will appear when it is released.” over vague tide metaphors.\n+2. **Suggest wonder instead of announcing it.** Do not rely on “epic,” “magical,” “incredible,” “immersive,” or “unforgettable.” Prefer “A new mark has appeared on the chart.”\n+3. **Nautical texture, not pirate cosplay.** Use nautical vocabulary when it improves identity and comprehension. Never use fake pirate dialect in platform voice.\n+4. **Emotion is observed, not commanded.** Present evidence and trust the player. Do not prescribe the emotion they should feel.\n+5. **The player is capable and must never be shamed.** Hints, recovery, wrong answers, and verification failures preserve dignity and support progress.\n+6. **Humor creates breathing room.** Humor is dry, restrained, and situational. It may appear in harmless moments, never in security, privacy, billing, or data-loss states.\n+\n+## 4. The six voice layers\n+\n+### 4.1 Voyagewright brand voice\n+\n+For product sites, marketing, trailers, onboarding, descriptions, and public empty states. It may be poetic but must explain the product. Examples: “Create stories worth entering.” “Build a Chronicle. Gather your Crew. Turn an ordinary game night into a Voyage they will remember.” “Your games already provide the world. Voyagewright gives that world a story made for your Crew.”\n+\n+### 4.2 Platform voice\n+\n+For navigation, authentication, invitations, loading states, settings, and system notifications. It is neutral, concise, lightly atmospheric, and never pretends to be a story character. Examples: “Invitation accepted.” “Your Voyage is ready.” “Progress saved.” “The next Chapter has not been released.” “No active Voyages.”\n+\n+### 4.3 Chronicle narrator\n+\n+Chronicles own their narrators and characters. Narration, journals, letters, dialogue, found documents, and Chronicle-specific prompts may be authored by a Captain, scientist, ghost, archivist, dispatcher, unreliable narrator, or no narrator. Voyagewright does not force nautical narration into non-nautical Chronicles, and platform errors never masquerade as Chronicle prose.\n+\n+### 4.4 Player experience voice\n+\n+For Chapter releases, Artifact reveals, progress framing, Waypoint updates, and collection states. **Functional truth comes first; atmospheric meaning follows.**\n+\n+- “Chapter III released. A new entry has opened in the journal.”\n+- “Artifact recovered. The sea-glass lens has been added to your collection.”\n+- “A new Echo was found. Some places keep more than treasure.”\n+\n+### 4.5 Captain interface voice\n+\n+For session controls, approvals, hints, player status, Chapter releases, and live recovery. It is operational, fast, and unambiguous. Examples: “Release Chapter III,” “Awaiting player confirmation,” “Approve discovery,” “Send Hint 2,” “Pause Voyage,” and “The player is offline. This action will be delivered when they reconnect.”\n+\n+### 4.6 Studio voice\n+\n+For authoring, validation, publishing, versioning, assets, and preview. It is creator-to-creator, encouraging, structurally intelligent, and comfortable with ordinary design terms. Examples: “Create Chronicle,” “Add Chapter,” “Add Passage,” “Preview from here,” and “Chapter IV cannot be published because it has no completion path.” Publishing creates a permanent version; existing Voyages continue using their current version.\n+\n+**Boundary rule:** every line has a known owner. System copy, Captain instructions, Chronicle narration, character dialogue, private creator notes, and user-generated content must not leak into one another.\n+\n+## 5. Error and recovery language\n+\n+Errors are calm, specific, honest, and actionable. Each recovery message answers: what happened; whether anything was lost; what the system is doing now; and what the user can do next.\n+\n+| Situation | Heading | Supporting copy |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Connection loss | Connection lost | Your progress is safe. Voyagewright is trying to reconnect. |\n+| Failed reconnect | Unable to reconnect | Your progress is saved through Chapter II. Check your connection, then try again. |\n+| Expired invitation | This invitation has expired | Ask the Captain to create a new invitation. |\n+| Publishing problem | The Chronicle was not published | Chapter III contains a Passage with no valid destination. Your draft has been preserved. |\n+| Unsupported content | This Chronicle requires a newer version of Voyagewright | Update the application before continuing. Your Voyage has not advanced. |\n+| Verification failure | The object could not be confirmed | Adjust the view so the full marker is visible, then try again. |\n+\n+Prefer “The Chronicle could not be opened. Your progress has not changed. Try again, or return to the Library.” Never leave the user with only “Something went wrong.”\n+\n+## 6. Canonical vocabulary\n+\n+| Concept | Canonical term | Usage |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Authored playable experience | Chronicle | Central content object created in Studio |\n+| Active playthrough | Voyage | Live or resumable instance of a Chronicle |\n+| Person running the experience | Captain | Operator guiding a Voyage |\n+| Participating players | Crew | People taking part in the Voyage |\n+| Major story division | Chapter | Large authored section |\n+| Individual authored unit | Passage | Narration, objective, riddle, choice, reveal, checkpoint, or transition |\n+| Location or progression point | Waypoint | Destination, checkpoint, or meaningful position |\n+| Optional discovered memory | Echo | Side discovery that deepens context without blocking progress |\n+| Significant collected object | Artifact | Discovered object with narrative meaning |\n+| Completed-session record | Voyage Record | Durable history of an ended or completed Voyage |\n+| Creator application | Voyagewright Studio | Authoring and publishing interface |\n+| Player application | Voyagewright Player | Player-facing experience |\n+| Session-control application | Captain's Console | Live operating interface |\n+| Test playthrough | Preview Voyage | Controlled test run |\n+| Completed or retired content storage | Archive | Clear ordinary storage term |\n+\n+Creators see **Passage** even when an internal type is named `StoryBlock`. A Passage can contain narration, a riddle, an objective, a confirmation, an Artifact reveal, an image transformation, a choice, a Captain checkpoint, or a Chapter ending. Keep ordinary clear words literal: Save, Cancel, Delete, Email, Password, Privacy, Version history, and Validation errors.\n+\n+## 7. Required microcopy transformations\n+\n+| Generic or inherited language | Voyagewright language |\n+| --- | --- |\n+| Start Tall Tale | Begin Chronicle |\n+| Active game session | Active Voyage |\n+| Create new story | Create Chronicle |\n+| Game master dashboard | Captain's Console |\n+| Continue | Continue Voyage |\n+| Submit | Confirm Answer |\n+| You unlocked an item! | Artifact recovered |\n+| Waiting for admin | Awaiting the Captain |\n+| No data | Nothing has been recorded yet |\n+| Replay animation | Replay Presentation |\n+| Go back | Return to Library |\n+| End session | End Voyage |\n+| Duplicate campaign | Create a new Voyage from this Chronicle |\n+| Error loading story | This Chronicle could not be opened |\n+| Testing mode | Preview Voyage |\n+| Draft game | Draft Chronicle |\n+\n+For important Player events, use functional truth first and atmospheric meaning second.\n+\n+## 8. Sentence-level standards\n+\n+Use sentence case for buttons, labels, dialogs, and settings; title case only for proper product names, Chronicle titles, Chapter titles, named places, and proper nouns. Prefer active voice, short labels, natural contractions, direct second person, and concrete verbs. Use exclamation marks rarely. Use an ellipsis only for actual ongoing work. Name the object rather than referring to “it.” Do not narrate an internal emotion the user is supposed to feel. Avoid decorative punctuation.\n+\n+## 9. Language across the emotional arc\n+\n+Early Chronicles may be energetic, curious, and lightly playful. Middle movement becomes observant and widening. Reflection Chapters are quiet and concrete. Finales are minimal and spacious: no marketing language, emotional instructions, or unnecessary explanation. **Reveal the thing; do not explain the correct emotional response to the thing.**\n+\n+## 10. Speaker ownership\n+\n+Every meaningful line needs a known speaker, audience, and delivery context. Recommended fields are `speakerType`, `speakerId`, `voiceProfile`, `deliveryContext`, `audience`, `tone`, `visibility`, `localizationKey`, and `contentVersion`.\n+\n+Recommended speaker types are `SYSTEM`, `NARRATOR`, `CHARACTER`, `CAPTAIN_OPERATOR`, `CREATOR`, `PLAYER`, and `UNATTRIBUTED_DOCUMENT`. The interface must know whether a line is platform-owned, Chronicle-owned, or user-generated, and must prevent system errors, private Captain notes, and narrator voices from crossing their audience boundary.\n+\n+## 11. Nautical intensity\n+\n+Brand and marketing can use roughly 40% nautical texture; product navigation and terminology about 25%; ordinary controls about 10%; Captain's Console about 15%; Studio about 5–10%; and errors, security, privacy, and billing 0%. Chronicle content is author-defined. The nautical metaphor supports many genres; fake pirate speech does not.\n+\n+## 12. Accessibility and localization\n+\n+Atmosphere must never be the only carrier of status or instruction. Pair supporting poetry with literal headings when action matters, do not encode status through color or decoration alone, use labels that retain meaning outside their visual context, avoid idioms in security/privacy/verification/recovery, preserve semantic order in all motion modes, and keep announcements concise for screen readers.\n+\n+Store platform strings separately from Chronicle prose; use stable localization keys, not English strings, as identifiers; avoid concatenation that breaks grammar; allow layouts to expand; preserve speaker and tone metadata; do not translate names or invented Chronicle terms without creator intent; and provide translator context, audience, and speaker information. Literal language overrides atmosphere for safety, privacy, data loss, payment, and account access.\n+\n+## 13. Copy governance\n+\n+The workflow requires a canonical terminology registry, screen-by-screen inventory with ownership and status, reusable patterns for recovery/confirmation/loading/empty states/hints/invitations, a Chronicle voice profile schema, a copy review checklist in PR and release review, automated banned-term detection, and versioned migration notes.\n+\n+Review in this order: confirm speaker and audience; write functional truth plainly; add only useful atmosphere; check the glossary; check the action label alone; verify impact and next action for recovery; test motion/mobile/screen-reader presentation; and read the line in the complete user flow.\n+\n+## 14. Definitive voice statement\n+\n+Voyagewright is warm, observant, precise, and quietly imaginative. It uses nautical language as texture rather than costume. It gives clear direction, trusts the player's intelligence, protects dignity, and never explains an emotion that the experience can earn. The platform speaks as a guide; the Chronicle speaks through its author and characters.\n+\n+When two versions are equally clear, choose the more human one. When one is clearer, choose clarity. When neither is clear, stop decorating the sentence and fix the sentence.\n+\n+## Appendix A. Sample copy library\n+\n+| Area | Heading or action | Supporting copy |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Onboarding | Welcome to Voyagewright | Create a Chronicle, gather your Crew, and turn a familiar world into a story made for them. |\n+| Onboarding | Choose your role | Join as a Player, guide the Voyage as Captain, or create in Studio. |\n+| Onboarding | No account yet? | Create one to save your Chronicles, Voyages, and invitations. |\n+| Invitations | You've been invited | A Captain has invited you to join a Voyage. |\n+| Invitations | Invitation accepted | Your place in the Crew is confirmed. |\n+| Player progression | Chapter released | A new entry has opened in the journal. |\n+| Player progression | Waypoint revealed | A new bearing has been marked on the chart. |\n+| Player progression | Artifact recovered | The object has been added to your collection. |\n+| Player progression | Echo discovered | This memory is optional, but it may change what the journey means. |\n+| Player progression | Awaiting the Captain | Your progress is saved. The Voyage will continue when the next Passage is released. |\n+| Captain controls | Release Chapter | Make the next Chapter available to the Crew. |\n+| Captain controls | Approve discovery | Confirm the player's submission and advance the Voyage. |\n+| Captain controls | Send hint | Reveal the selected hint to the player. |\n+| Captain controls | Pause Voyage | Players can keep reading, but progression controls will be suspended. |\n+| Captain controls | End Voyage | The current Voyage will close. Its record and progress will be preserved. |\n+| Studio | Create Chronicle | Start with a title, premise, and first Chapter. |\n+| Studio | Add Passage | Choose narration, objective, riddle, choice, reveal, or Captain checkpoint. |\n+| Studio | Preview from here | Run the Chronicle from this Passage without changing the draft. |\n+| Studio | Draft saved | Your latest changes are preserved. |\n+| Studio | Cannot publish yet | Two Passages have no valid destination. |\n+| Empty state | No Chronicles yet | Every Voyage starts with a first page. |\n+| Empty state | No active Voyages | Join an invitation or begin one from your Chronicle Library. |\n+| Recovery | Connection lost | Your progress is safe. Voyagewright is trying to reconnect. |\n+| Recovery | Unable to reconnect | Your progress is preserved. Check your connection, then try again. |\n+| Recovery | Upload incomplete | The file was not saved. Choose it again after checking your connection. |\n+\n+## Appendix B. Copy review checklist\n+\n+- Speaker: speaker and audience are known; content class is known; no private or unpublished information leaks.\n+- Clarity: the user knows what happened and what to do next; heading and action stand alone.\n+- Voice: warm, precise, restrained; nautical texture supports comprehension; the user is trusted; emotion is not prescribed.\n+- Tone: serious states have no humor; Captain actions are direct; finales are quieter rather than louder.\n+- Terminology: the canonical glossary is used; inherited Tall Tale language is removed from platform copy; literal controls remain literal; Chronicle, Voyage, Chapter, Passage, Waypoint, Echo, and Artifact are consistent.\n+- Accessibility and localization: screen-reader meaning is complete; color/animation/iconography is not the only meaning; strings survive translation; translation context identifies speaker, audience, and delivery context.\n+- Recovery: impact, current system action, concrete next step, and dignity are present.\n+\n+A line is ready when it is clear without context, fluent within context, owned by the correct speaker, consistent with the glossary, and appropriate for accessibility, localization, and the emotional moment.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Language\\Development_Docs\\Voyagewright_Universal_Language_Migration_Record.md\n+# Voyagewright Universal Language Migration Record\n+\n+## Status\n+\n+**Branch:** `development/universal-language`  \n+**Dedicated worktree:** `Forever-Treasure-Language`  \n+**Governing document:** `Voyagewright_Language_Design_Foundation.pdf` (Foundation v1.0, July 2026; SHA-256 `9D9668909CD448A9E471D0D62457498D0865CDF062D972D4612EB539FB40B46C`)  \n+**Starting committed baseline:** `7c3677035867081e4078536bef2f7d540bfd94e6` (`work/lanternwake-latest` remote state)  \n+**Implementation state:** In progress — glossary and copy architecture established; product-surface audit and migration follow.\n+\n+## Authority and change protection\n+\n+Project Lanternwake owns component and scene architecture, animation runtimes, scene hosts, lifecycle behavior, transitions, motion accessibility, and presentation mechanics. This branch owns visible product language only. Copy edits must preserve all existing behavior and animation hooks.\n+\n+The active Lanternwake checkout is `forever-treasure-companion` on `work/lanternwake-latest` at `ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04`; it was not modified. A separate Phase 4 worktree contains concurrent uncommitted lifecycle changes in invitation, sign-in, voyage-room, and player-experience files. Static wording in those files is an integration-risk area and must be reconciled against their completed committed state before final integration.\n+\n+## Canonical terminology and capitalization\n+\n+| Concept | Canonical display term |\n+| --- | --- |\n+| Company | Absolute Relative Systems |\n+| Product | Voyagewright |\n+| Creator interface | Voyagewright Studio |\n+| Player interface | Voyagewright Player |\n+| Live operator interface | Captain's Console |\n+| Connector | Voyagewright Connector |\n+| Authored experience | Chronicle |\n+| Playthrough | Voyage |\n+| Operator | Captain |\n+| Participants | Crew |\n+| Major division | Chapter |\n+| Authored unit | Passage |\n+| Progress point | Waypoint |\n+| Optional discovery | Echo |\n+| Major collected object | Artifact |\n+| Test playthrough | Preview Voyage |\n+| Completed record | Voyage Record |\n+| Creator collection | Chronicle Library |\n+| Active-play collection | Active Voyages |\n+| Completed-play collection | Voyage History / Voyage Records |\n+\n+Capitalize canonical product objects when they name Voyagewright concepts. Use ordinary sentence case when the same words describe a non-product concept. Preserve literal names for ordinary controls such as Save, Cancel, Delete, Email, Password, Privacy, Version history, and Validation errors.\n+\n+## Language architecture\n+\n+| Artifact | Purpose |\n+| --- | --- |\n+| `src/language/canonical-terms.ts` | Canonical terms, deprecated display terms, capitalization policy |\n+| `src/language/copy-types.ts` | Typed speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, voice layer, localization key |\n+| `src/language/platform-copy.ts` | Shared platform labels, empty/loading/recovery language |\n+| `src/language/player-copy.ts` | Functional-truth-first Player progression copy |\n+| `src/language/captain-copy.ts` | Direct Captain control and consequence language |\n+| `src/language/studio-copy.ts` | Creator-focused Studio labels |\n+| `src/language/error-copy.ts` | Parameterized, actionable error patterns |\n+\n+The product currently has no localization framework. Each new shared copy entry therefore carries a stable `localizationKey`, speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, and voice-layer metadata so localization can be introduced without using English copy as an identifier.\n+\n+## Audit coverage and application status\n+\n+| Surface | Status | Evidence / disposition |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Absolute Relative Systems public website | NOT PRESENT | No separate company-site package or deployment surface exists in this repository. |\n+| Voyagewright marketing site | IN PROGRESS | Root landing page and shared product shell are in `src/components/landing` and `src/components/shell`. |\n+| Voyagewright Player | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/player`, `src/components/player`, and `src/components/platform`. |\n+| Captain's Console | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/captain`, `src/components/captain`, `src/components/platform`. |\n+| Voyagewright Studio | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/studio`, `src/components/studio`, and `src/tall-tale`. |\n+| Voyagewright desktop wrapper | NOT PRESENT | No Electron, Tauri, native wrapper, shortcut, or desktop manifest is tracked. |\n+| Voyagewright Connector | NOT PRESENT | No connector application, tray surface, or capture-permission package is tracked. |\n+| Installer / updater | NOT PRESENT | No installer or update configuration is tracked. |\n+| Emails | NOT PRESENT | No email template or delivery system is tracked. |\n+| Notifications | PARTIAL | In-app state and status notifications are audited with their owning Player/Captain flows. No separate OS notification service is tracked. |\n+| Metadata and manifests | IN PROGRESS | Next metadata is in `src/app/layout.tsx`; no PWA manifest is tracked. |\n+| Documentation | IN PROGRESS | This record, foundation companion, inventory, and validator documentation are the owned documentation surfaces. |\n+\n+## Technical legacy and intentional preservation\n+\n+| Area | Decision | Reason |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| `campaignId`, `campaignSlug`, `storyBlock`, existing API route names | Preserve internally | Persisted data/API compatibility and low-risk migration boundary; must not appear in product-facing copy. |\n+| `tall-tale` CSS/data identifiers and implementation file names | Preserve internally | Animation/style/runtime identifiers are Lanternwake-adjacent technical legacy, not product language. |\n+| Chronicle-authored narration, dialogue, riddles, letters, and documents | Preserve | Chronicle authors own their voice; only accidental product-language leakage or placeholder copy is migrated. |\n+| `/tales`, `/play`, `/tale`, `/quartermaster`, and API routes | Preserve for this branch | Route migration requires redirects, saved-link analysis, and coordinated integration; visible labels and metadata migrate now. |\n+\n+## Validation plan\n+\n+1. Run the forbidden-language validator against production copy roots after each copy batch.\n+2. Run copy-registry and feature tests after each owned surface changes.\n+3. Run formatting, lint, typecheck, unit tests, language validation, build, and the isolated repository validation harness serially.\n+4. Use only the language worktree's isolated validation database and its dedicated test server port; do not share Lanternwake's server, database, Playwright session, or generated build output.\n+5. Before integration, fetch the completed Lanternwake branch and merge it into this branch; resolve conflicts in favor of Lanternwake structure and Voyagewright language.\n+\n+## Completion checklist\n+\n+- [x] Authoritative PDF located, read, checksum-recorded, and preserved in this worktree.\n+- [x] Searchable Markdown companion created.\n+- [x] Migration record and initial inventory structure created.\n+- [x] Canonical glossary, ownership metadata, and feature copy catalogs created.\n+- [ ] Production copy migration complete.\n+- [ ] Forbidden-term validator and validation-pipeline integration complete.\n+- [ ] All BLOCKER/HIGH inventory items resolved.\n+- [ ] Full validation complete.\n+- [ ] Lanternwake baseline synchronization complete.\n+- [ ] **Subagents started — 3 lanes (all available worker slots are now in use).**

| Lane | Mode | Owned surface | Deliverable | Guardrails |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Player | Write | Player routes, libraries, access, and tale catalog | Migrated Player copy plus focused tests and one commit | Excludes Lanternwake-lifecycle files and all shared language/validation files |
| Captain | Write | Captain, Quartermaster/GM, and related client-facing API copy | Operational Console copy plus focused tests and one commit | Preserves command/API behavior; excludes shared architecture |
| Studio | Write | Studio routes/components, registry, validation, publishing, and Studio APIs | Creator-focused copy plus focused tests and one commit | Preserves internal models, DnD, animation, and API paths |

I remain the integration owner: shared catalog/enforcement, landing/navigation/metadata, protected overlap files, documentation, isolated validation, and final Git review. No lane owns the active Lanternwake worktree, server ports, database, generated assets, or full validation.
