<p>{role.copy}</p>\r\n                {role.id === \"captain\" && status?.captain.authenticated && (\r\n                  <small>\r\n                    {status.captain.waitingPlayers ?? 0} {status.captain.waitingPlayers === 1 ? \"Player\" : \"Players\"}{\" \"}\r\n                    awaiting launch\r\n                  </small>\r\n                )}\r\n                {role.id === \"creator\" && status?.creator.authenticated && status.creator.recentDraft && (\r\n                  <small>Recent draft: {status.creator.recentDraft.title}</small>\r\n                )}\r\n                <Link\r\n                  className=\"role-entry\"\r\n                  href={next.href}\r\n                  aria-describedby={descriptionId}\r\n                  onClick={() => setSelectedRole(role.id)}\r\n                >\r\n                  {next.label}\r\n                </Link>\r\n                <span id={descriptionId} className=\"sr-only\">\r\n                  {role.copy}\r\n                </span>\r\n              </motion.article>\r\n            );\r\n          })}\r\n        </div>\r\n        <Link className=\"invitation-code-link\" href=\"/player/sign-in#invitation-code\">\r\n          Have an invitation code?\r\n        </Link>\r\n      </section>\r\n      <section className=\"gateway-explainer\" aria-labelledby={explainerTitleId}>\r\n        <header>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">A story your group can step into</p>\r\n          <h2 id={explainerTitleId}>What is a Tall Tale?</h2>\r\n          <p>\r\n            A Tall Tale is a guided interactive experience made of chapters, prompts, choices, activities, and reveals.\r\n            It can support a game night, celebration, trip, reunion, date, family adventure, or an entirely custom\r\n            story.\r\n          </p>\r\n        </header>\r\n        <div className=\"gateway-how-grid\">\r\n          <article>\r\n            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">01</span>\r\n            <h3>Choose an adventure</h3>\r\n            <p>Browse published Tall Tales and understand the time, group size, and story before you begin.</p>\r\n          </article>\r\n          <article>\r\n            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">02</span>\r\n            <h3>Gather participants</h3>\r\n            <p>A Captain configures the voyage and sends each Player a private invitation link or short code.</p>\r\n          </article>\r\n          <article>\r\n            <span aria-hidden=\"true\">03</span>\r\n            <h3>Continue your story</h3>\r\n            <p>\r\n              The journal preserves live progress, reconnects safely, and keeps completed editions available to revisit.\r\n            </p>\r\n          </article>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div className=\"gateway-trust-panel\">\r\n          <div>\r\n            <p className=\"eyebrow\">Designed around your group</p>\r\n            <h3>Flexible by default, personal when you choose</h3>\r\n            <p>\r\n              System controls stay inclusive and reusable. Personal details belong only to the Tall Tale, invitation, or\r\n              customization your group selected.\r\n            </p>\r\n          </div>\r\n          <ul aria-label=\"Experience examples\">\r\n            <li>Friends</li>\r\n            <li>Families</li>\r\n            <li>Celebrations</li>\r\n            <li>Game nights</li>\r\n            <li>Trips</li>\r\n            <li>Custom events</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </section>\r\n      <AnimationTestButton />\r\n    </>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\r\nimport { gmCan, requireGm } from \"@/lib/security\";\r\nimport { requirePlayerIdentity } from \"@/platform/auth\";\r\nimport { listPlayerLibrary } from \"@/platform/libraries\";\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\r\n\r\nexport async function GET() {\r\n  const [playerSession, staffSession] = await Promise.all([requirePlayerIdentity(), requireGm()]);\r\n  const player = playerSession ? await listPlayerLibrary(playerSession.playerProfileId) : null;\r\n  const captain = staffSession && gmCan(staffSession.user, \"CAPTAIN\");\r\n  const creator = staffSession && gmCan(staffSession.user, \"CREATE_TALES\");\r\n  const [waitingPlayers, recentDraft] = await Promise.all([\r\n    captain\r\n      ? db.playthroughMembership.count({\r\n          where: {\r\n            status: \"READY\",\r\n            playthrough: { previewMode: false, OR: [{ captainId: staffSession.userId }, { captainId: null }] },\r\n          },\r\n        })\r\n      : 0,\r\n    creator\r\n      ? db.tallTale.findFirst({\r\n          where: { creatorId: staffSession.userId, archivedAt: null },\r\n          orderBy: { updatedAt: \"desc\" },\r\n          select: { id: true, title: true },\r\n        })\r\n      : null,\r\n  ]);\r\n  const activePlayerCards = player ? [...player.groups.inProgress, ...player.groups.awaitingCaptain] : [];\r\n  return NextResponse.json({\r\n    player: playerSession\r\n      ? {\r\n          authenticated: true,\r\n          displayName: playerSession.player.displayName,\r\n          activeCount: activePlayerCards.length,\r\n          continue:\r\n            activePlayerCards.length === 1\r\n              ? { label: `Continue ${activePlayerCards[0].title}`, href: activePlayerCards[0].primaryHref }\r\n              : { label: \"Open My Tall Tale Library\", href: \"/player/library\" },\r\n        }\r\n      : { authenticated: false },\r\n    captain: captain\r\n      ? {\r\n          authenticated: true,\r\n          waitingPlayers,\r\n          continue: { label: \"Return to Captain's Command\", href: \"/captain/library\" },\r\n        }\r\n      : { authenticated: false },\r\n    creator: creator\r\n      ? {\r\n          authenticated: true,\r\n          recentDraft,\r\n          continue: recentDraft\r\n            ? { label: `Continue Editing ${recentDraft.title}`, href: `/studio/tales/${recentDraft.id}` }\r\n            : { label: \"Open Studio Library\", href: \"/studio/library\" },\r\n        }\r\n      : { authenticated: false },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

sceneHost, targetsReady]);\\n\\n  function continuation(role: (typeof roles)[number]) {\\n    const current = status?.[role.id];\\n    return current?.authenticated && current.continue\\n      ? current.continue\\n      : { label: `Enter as ${role.title}`, href: role.href };\\n  }\\n\\n  const replay = () => {\\n    if (!root.current || !sceneHost || !targetsReady) return;\\n    presentationAbortRef.current?.abort();\\n    const presentationAbort = new AbortController();\\n    presentationAbortRef.current = presentationAbort;\\n    void director\\n      .play(\\\"first-arrival\\\", {\\n        root: root.current,\\n        queue: false,\\n        sceneHost,\\n        hostId: sceneHost.hostId,\\n        hostKind: sceneHost.kind,\\n        requestSource: \\\"replay\\\",\\n        signal: presentationAbort.signal,\\n      })\\n      .then(reportNonPresentedReceipt)\\n      .catch(() => undefined)\\n      .finally(() => {\\n        if (presentationAbortRef.current === presentationAbort) presentationAbortRef.current = null;\\n      });\\n  };\\n\\n  return (\\n    <>\\n      <span ref={bindRootSentinel} hidden />\\n      <div ref={bindSky} className=\\\"harbor-sky\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"sky\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\n        <div ref={bindStars} className=\\\"star-field\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"stars\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          {starStyles.map((style, index) => (\\n            <i key={index} style={style} />\\n          ))}\\n        </div>\\n        <div ref={bindMoon} className=\\\"moon-glow\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"moon\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          <i />\\n        </div>\\n        <div ref={bindFogBack} className=\\\"distant-clouds\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"fog-back\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\" />\\n      </div>\\n      <div\\n        ref={bindHorizon}\\n        className=\\\"harbor-horizon\\\"\\n        data-scene-part=\\\"horizon\\\"\\n        data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\"\\n        aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\"\\n      >\\n        <div ref={bindShip} className=\\\"harbor-ship-arrival-target\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"ship\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          <div className=\\\"distant-ship\\\" data-parallax-layer=\\\"ship\\\">\\n            <i />\\n            <i />\\n          </div>\\n        </div>\\n      </div>\\n      <div ref={bindOcean} className=\\\"harbor-waves\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"ocean\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n        <LottieEffect asset={lottieAssets.moonlitWaves} mode={mode} label=\\\"Moonlight moving across the harbor\\\" />\\n      </div>\\n      <div ref={bindFogFront} className=\\\"harbor-fog\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"fog-front\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n        <LottieEffect asset={lottieAssets.rollingFog} mode={mode} label=\\\"Fog rolling over the harbor\\\" />\\n      </div>\\n      <div className=\\\"foreground-dock\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\n        <span className=\\\"dock-rope\\\" />\\n      </div>\\n      <div className=\\\"hanging-lantern\\\" data-ambient=\\\"lantern\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\n        <i />\\n      </div>\\n      <div\\n        ref={bindNauticalBorder}\\n        className=\\\"nautical-border\\\"\\n        data-scene-part=\\\"nautical-border\\\"\\n        data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\"\\n        aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\"\\n      />\\n\\n      <section className=\\\"harbor-content gateway-content\\\" aria-labelledby={gatewayTitleId}>\\n        <p ref={bindArrivalCopyEyebrow} className=\\\"eyebrow\\\" data-scene-part=\\\"arrival-copy\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          Voyagewright\\n        </p>\\n        <h1 ref={bindTitle} id={gatewayTitleId} data-scene-part=\\\"title\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          {platformCopy.chooseRole.value}\\n        </h1>\\n        <p ref={bindArrivalCopyBody} data-scene-part=\\\"arrival-copy\\\" data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\">\\n          {platformCopy.brandIntroduction.value}\\n        </p>\\n        <div\\n          ref={bindArrivalActionPrimary}\\n          className=\\\"gateway-primary-actions\\\"\\n          data-scene-part=\\\"arrival-action\\\"\\n          data-runtime-boundary=\\\"gsap\\\"\\n        >\\n          <Link className=\\\"brass-button\\\" href=\\\"/tales\\\">\\n            {platformCopy.exploreChronicles.value}\\n          </Link>\\n          <Link className=\\\"button-secondary\\\" href=\\\"/player/sign-in#invitation-code\\\">\\n            Join with an Invitation\\n          </Link>\\n        </div>\\n        <div className=\\\"landing-controls\\\" aria-label=\\\"Opening presentation controls\\\">\\n          <button onClick={replay} disabled={snapshot.isPlaying || !targetsReady || !sceneHost}>\\n            Replay presentation\\n          </button>\\n          {snapshot.isPlaying && snapshot.label !== \\\"dark-sea\\\" && (\\n            <button onClick={() => director.skip()}>Skip opening presentation</button>\\n          )}\\n          <button onClick={cycle} aria-label={`Motion: ${mode}. (current?.authenticated ? \\\"Session remembered\\\" : \\\"Adventure role\\\") : \\\"Checking session…\\\"}\\n                </p>\\n                <h2>{role.title}</h2>\\n                <p>{role.copy}</p>\\n                {role.id === \\\"captain\\\" && status?.captain.authenticated && (\\n                  <small>\\n                    {status.captain.waitingPlayers ?? 0} {status.captain.waitingPlayers === 1 ? \\\"Player\\\" : \\\"Players\\\"}{\\\" \\\"}\\n                    awaiting a Voyage\\n                  </small>\\n                )}\\n                {role.id === \\\"creator\\\" && status?.creator.authenticated && status.creator.recentDraft && (\\n                  <small>Recent draft: {status.creator.recentDraft.title}</small>\\n                )}\\n \"Captain's Console\" : \"Voyagewright Studio\";\r\n  const destination = intent === \"captain\" ? \"/captain/library\" : \"/studio/library\";\r\n  const busy = asyncState.busy;\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const update = () => setOnline(navigator.onLine);\r\n    update();\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"online\", update);\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"offline\", update);\r\n    return () => {\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"online\", update);\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"offline\", update);\r\n    };\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  async function handOffRoute(signal: AbortSignal) {\r\n    if (onRouteHandoff) return onRouteHandoff(destination, signal);\r\n    router.push(destination);\r\n    router.refresh();\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function restoreAfterFailure(run: AuthoritativeAsyncRun, message: string) {\r\n    if (!asyncState.fail(run)) return;\r\n    setStatus(\"\");\r\n    setError(message);\r\n    setCeremonyState(\"rejected\");\r\n    window.requestAnimationFrame(() => usernameInput.current?.focus());\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  async function submit(event: React.FormEvent) {\r\n    event.preventDefault();\r\n    const run = asyncState.begin();\r\n    if (!run) return;\r\n    setOperationKey(run.id);\r\n    setError(\"\");\r\n    setStatus(\"\");\r\n    setCeremonyState(\"pending\");\r\n    let credentialsAccepted = false;\r\n    try {\r\n      const response = await fetch(\"/api/gm/login\", {\r\n        method: \"POST\",\r\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),\r\n        signal: run.controller.signal,\r\n      });\r\n      if (!response.ok) {\r\n        const body = await parseJsonResponse<{ error?: string }>(response);\r\n        return restoreAfterFailure(run, body?.error ?? \"Sign-in failed. Please try again.\");\r\n      }\r\n\r\n      const statusResponse = await fetch(\"/api/gateway/status\", {\r\n        cache: \"no-store\",\r\n        signal: run.controller.signal,\r\n      });\r\n      const gatewayStatus = await parseJsonResponse<GatewayStatus>(statusResponse);\r\n      if (!statusResponse.ok || !gatewayStatus) {\r\n        return restoreAfterFailure(\r\n          run,\r\n          gatewayStatus?.error ?? `Sign-in succeeded, but ${title} permission could not be verified. Please try again.`,\r\n        );\r\n      }\r\n      if (!gatewayStatus[intent]?.authenticated) {\r\n        return restoreAfterFailure(\r\n          run,\r\n          `This account is signed in but does not have ${intent === \"captain\" ? \"Captain\" : \"Creator\"} permission.`,\r\n        );\r\n      }\r\n\r\n      if (!asyncState.succeed(run)) return;\r\n      credentialsAccepted = true;\r\n      setCeremonyState(\"accepted\");\r\n      setStatus(`Sign-in accepted. Opening ${title}.`);\r\n      await handOffRoute(run.controller.signal);\r\n    } catch {\r\n      restoreAfterFailure(\r\n        run,\r\n        credentialsAccepted\r\n          ? `Sign-in succeeded, but ${title} could not be opened. Please try again.`\r\n          : `${title} could not be reached. Check that the app is running and try again.`,\r\n      );\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <main\r\n      className={`platform-auth staff-auth-page intent-${intent}`}\r\n      data-auth-state={ceremonyState}\r\n      data-async-state={asyncState.phase}\r\n      data-auth-operation={operationKey}\r\n    >\r\n      <StaffCeremonyBoundary intent={intent} mode={mode} operationKey={operationKey} state={ceremonyState} />\r\n      <section className=\"auth-ledger\" aria-labelledby=\"staff-sign-in-title\">\r\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\">{intent === \"captain\" ? \"Operational waters\" : \"Authoring waters\"}</p>\r\n        <h1 id=\"staff-sign-in-title\">Enter {title}</h1>\r\n        <p>\r\n          {intent === \"captain\"\r\n            ? \"Guide live voyages and manage secure invitations.\"\r\n            : \"Author, validate, publish, compare, and preserve immutable editions.\"}\r\n        </p>\r\n        {!online && (\r\n          <p className=\"platform-status auth-offline\" role=\"status\">\r\n            Offline. Entered credentials are preserved; reconnect before submitting.\r\n          </p>\r\n        )}\r\n        {authorized ? (\r\n          <Link className=\"brass-button auth-continue\" href={destination}>\r\n            Continue to {title}\r\n          </Link>\r\n        ) : signedIn ? (\r\n          <p className=\"platform-error\" role=\"alert\">\r\n            Your current account is not authorized for this workspace. Return to a role you are allowed to use.\r\n          </p>\r\n        ) : null}\r\n        <form onSubmit={submit} aria-busy={busy} aria-describedby={error ? \"staff-sign-in-error\" : undefined}>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Username</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              ref={usernameInput}\r\n              autoComplete=\"username\"\r\n              value={username}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setUsername(event.target.value)}\r\n              required\r\n            />\r\n          </label>\r\n          <label>\r\n            <span>Password</span>\r\n            <input\r\n              type=\"password\"\r\n              autoComplete=\"current-password\"\r\n              value={password}\r\n              onChange={(event) => setPassword(event.target.value)}\r\n              required\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

  },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.playthroughMembership.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        playthroughId: session.id,\r\n        playerProfileId: player.id,\r\n        status: \"ACTIVE_MEMBER\",\r\n first.id,\r\n      idempotencyKey: `${key}:session`,\r\n      payload: { taleId: tale.id, versionId: version.id },\r\n      correlationId: key,\r\n    });\r\n    const event = await enterBlock(tx, session, snapshot, first, \"player\", null, key);\r\n    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        actorType: \"ANONYMOUS\",\r\n        action: \"COMPATIBILITY_PLAYTHROUGH_STARTED\",\r\n        resourceType: \"PLAYTHROUGH\",\r\n        resourceId: session.id,\r\n        correlationId: key,\r\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ taleId: tale.id, versionId: version.id }),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    return { session, event };\r\n  });\r\n  emit(created.session.id, created.event);\r\n  logger.info(\r\n    { area: \"tall-tale-session\", sessionId: created.session.id, taleId: tale.id, versionId: version.id },\r\n    \"Published Tall Tale session started\",\r\n  );\r\n  return { sessionId: created.session.id, token, taleSlug: slug, versionId: version.id };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function launchTalePlaythrough(sessionId: string, actorId: string, expectedVersion?: number) {\r\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n    where: { id: sessionId },\r\n    include: { version: true, memberships: true, invitations: true },\r\n  });\r\n  if (session.previewMode || !session.version) throw new Error(\"Only a published real voyage can be launched.\");\r\n  if (session.captainId && session.captainId !== actorId)\r\n    throw new Error(\"This voyage is assigned to another Captain.\");\r\n  if (![\"READY\", \"SCHEDULED\"].includes(session.status))\r\n    throw new Error(`This voyage is ${session.status.toLocaleLowerCase()} and is not ready to launch.`);\r\n    select: { id: true, taleId: true, status: true, previewMode: true, accessTokenHash: true, completedAt: true },\r\n  });\r\n  if (!session || session.previewMode || digest(token) !== session.accessTokenHash) return null;\r\n  return {\r\n    sessionId: session.id,\r\n    taleId: session.taleId,\r\n    status: session.completedAt || session.status === \"COMPLETED\" ? \"COMPLETED\" : session.status,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function startPreviewSession(taleId: string, creatorId: string, startBlockId?: string) {\r\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\r\n  const snapshot = snapshotFromStudio(studio);\r\n  const first = blockById(snapshot, startBlockId ?? null) ?? blocksOf(snapshot)[0];\r\n  if (!first) throw new Error(\"Add a story block before opening preview mode.\");\r\n  const token = randomBytes(32).toString(\"base64url\");\r\n  const key = randomUUID();\r\n  const created = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const session = await tx.taleSession.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        taleId,\r\n        ownerLabel: \"Studio preview\",\r\n        captainId: creatorId,\r\n        accessTokenHash: digest(token),\r\n        currentChapterId: chapterByBlock(snapshot, first.id)?.id,\r\n        currentBlockId: first.id,\r\n        previewMode: true,\r\n        draftRevisionId: studio.draft.id,\r\n        previewSnapshot: JSON.stringify(snapshot),\r\n        expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await appendEvent(tx, session, {\r\n      eventType: \"previewStarted\",\r\n      sourceType: \"studio\",\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function interactWithTaleSession(\r\n  sessionId: string,\r\n  token: string | undefined,\r\n  input: {\r\n    action: \"continue\" | \"confirm\" | \"answer\" | \"choice\" | \"timer\";\r\n    idempotencyKey: string;\r\n    answer?: string;\r\n    targetBlockId?: string;\r\n  },\r\n  authorizedPlayer = false,\r\n) {\r\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: sessionId }, include: { version: true } });\r\n  if (!authorizedPlayer && (!token || digest(token) !== session.accessTokenHash))\r\n    throw new Error(\"This voyage session is not available to this browser.\");\r\n  if (session.status !== \"ACTIVE\") throw new Error(`This session is ${session.status.toLowerCase()}.`);\r\n  const snapshot = snapshotOf(session);\r\n  const block = blockById(snapshot, session.currentBlockId);\r\n  if (!block) throw new Error(\"The current story block is unavailable.\");\r\n  const request = await db.taleVerificationRequest.findFirst({\r\n    where: { sessionId, blockId: block.id, status: \"PENDING\" },\r\n    orderBy: { requestedAt: \"desc\" },\r\n  });\r\n  if (input.action === \"answer\") {\r\n    if (!request || request.providerType !== \"textAnswer\") throw new Error(\"This block is not awaiting a text answer.\");\r\n    const config = parseJsonObject(request.configurationSnapshot);\r\n    const answer = normalized(input.answer ?? \"\", config);\r\n    const accepted =\r\n      Array.isArray(config.acceptedAnswers) &&\r\n      config.acceptedAnswers.some(\r\n        (candidate) => typeof candidate === \"string\" && normalized(candidate, config) === answer,\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 2.6 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 2.6 seconds\nOutput:\nimport { expect, test } from \"@playwright/test\";\n\nconst publicRoutes = [\"/\", \"/tales\", \"/player/sign-in\", \"/captain/sign-in\", \"/studio/sign-in\"] as const;\nconst prohibitedVisibleLanguage = [\n  /\\bTall Tales?\\b/iu,\n  /\\bcampaigns?\\b/iu,\n  /\\bgame sessions?\\b/iu,\n  /\\bgame masters?\\b/iu,\n  /\\bstory blocks?\\b/iu,\n  /\\bsomething went wrong\\b/iu,\n  /\\bunknown error\\b/iu,\n] as const;\n\ntest(\"public routes present Voyagewright language without inherited product terms\", async ({ page }) => {\n  for (const route of publicRoutes) {\n    const response = await page.goto(route);\n    expect(response?.ok(), `${route} should load`).toBe(true);\n    const visibleText = await page.locator(\"body\").innerText();\n    for (const prohibited of prohibitedVisibleLanguage) {\n      expect(visibleText, `${route} exposed ${prohibited}`).not.toMatch(prohibited);\n    }\n  }\n  await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/Voyagewright/u);\n});\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.5 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n# Voyagewright Universal Language Migration Record\n\n## Status\n\n**Branch:** `development/universal-language`\n**Dedicated worktree:** `Forever-Treasure-Language`\n**Governing document:** `Voyagewright_Language_Design_Foundation.pdf` (Foundation v1.0, July 2026; SHA-256 `9D9668909CD448A9E471D0D62457498D0865CDF062D972D4612EB539FB40B46C`)\n**Starting committed baseline:** `7c3677035867081e4078536bef2f7d540bfd94e6` (`work/lanternwake-latest` remote state)\n**Implementation state:** In progress â€” glossary and copy architecture established; product-surface audit and migration follow.\n\n## Authority and change protection\n\nProject Lanternwake owns component and scene architecture, animation runtimes, scene hosts, lifecycle behavior, transitions, motion accessibility, and presentation mechanics. This branch owns visible product language only. Copy edits must preserve all existing behavior and animation hooks.\n\nThe active Lanternwake checkout is `forever-treasure-companion` on `work/lanternwake-latest` at `ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04`; it was not modified. A separate Phase 4 worktree contains concurrent uncommitted lifecycle changes in invitation, sign-in, voyage-room, and player-experience files. Static wording in those files is an integration-risk area and must be reconciled against their completed committed state before final integration.\n\n## Canonical terminology and capitalization\n\n| Concept | Canonical display term |\n| --- | --- |\n| Company | Absolute Relative Systems |\n| Product | Voyagewright |\n| Creator interface | Voyagewright Studio |\n| Player interface | Voyagewright Player |\n| Live operator interface | Captain's Console |\n| Connector | Voyagewright Connector |\n| Authored experience | Chronicle |\n| Playthrough | Voyage |\n| Operator | Captain |\n| Participants | Crew |\n| Major division | Chapter |\n| Authored unit | Passage |\n| Progress point | Waypoint |\n| Optional discovery | Echo |\n| Major collected object | Artifact |\n| Test playthrough | Preview Voyage |\n| Completed record | Voyage Record |\n| Creator collection | Chronicle Library |\n| Active-play collection | Active Voyages |\n| Completed-play collection | Voyage History / Voyage Records |\n\nCapitalize canonical product objects when they name Voyagewright concepts. Use ordinary sentence case when the same words describe a non-product concept. Preserve literal names for ordinary controls such as Save, Cancel, Delete, Email, Password, Privacy, Version history, and Validation errors.\n\n## Language architecture\n\n| Artifact | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `src/language/canonical-terms.ts` | Canonical terms, deprecated display terms, capitalization policy |\n| `src/language/copy-types.ts` | Typed speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, voice layer, localization key |\n| `src/language/platform-copy.ts` | Shared platform labels, empty/loading/recovery language |\n| `src/language/player-copy.ts` | Functional-truth-first Player progression copy |\n| `src/language/captain-copy.ts` | Direct Captain control and consequence language |\n| `src/language/studio-copy.ts` | Creator-focused Studio labels |\n| `src/language/error-copy.ts` | Parameterized, actionable error patterns |\n\nThe product currently has no localization framework. Each new shared copy entry therefore carries a stable `localizationKey`, speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, and voice-layer metadata so localization can be introduced without using English copy as an identifier.\n\n## Audit coverage and application status\n\n| Surface | Status | Evidence / disposition |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Absolute Relative Systems public website | NOT PRESENT | No separate company-site package or deployment surface exists in this repository. |\n| Voyagewright marketing site | IN PROGRESS | Root landing page and shared product shell are in `src/components/landing` and `src/components/shell`. |\n| Voyagewright Player | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/player`, `src/components/player`, and `src/components/platform`. |\n| Captain's Console | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/captain`, `src/components/captain`, `src/components/platform`. |\n| Voyagewright Studio | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/studio`, `src/components/studio`, and `src/tall-tale`. |\n| Voyagewright desktop wrapper | NOT PRESENT | No Electron, Tauri, native wrapper, shortcut, or desktop manifest is tracked. |\n| Voyagewright Connector | NOT PRESENT | No connector application, tray surface, or capture-permission package is tracked. |\n| Installer / updater | NOT PRESENT | No installer or update configuration is tracked. |\n| Emails | NOT PRESENT | No email template or delivery system is tracked. |\n| Notifications | PARTIAL | In-app state and status notifications are audited with their owning Player/Captain flows. No separate OS notification service is tracked. |\n| Metadata and manifests | IN PROGRESS | Next metadata is in `src/app/layout.tsx`; no PWA manifest is tracked. |\n| Documentation | IN PROGRESS | This record, foundation companion, inventory, and validator documentation are the owned documentation surfaces. |\n\n## Technical legacy and intentional preservation\n\n| Area | Decision | Reason |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| `campaignId`, `campaignSlug`, `storyBlock`, existing API route names | Preserve internally | Persisted data/API compatibility and low-risk migration boundary; must not appear in product-facing copy. |\n| `tall-tale` CSS/data identifiers and implementation file names | Preserve internally | Animation/style/runtime identifiers are Lanternwake-adjacent technical legacy, not product language. |\n| Chronicle-authored narration, dialogue, riddles, letters, and documents | Preserve | Chronicle authors own their voice; only accidental product-language leakage or placeholder copy is migrated. |\n| `/tales`, `/play`, `/tale`, `/quartermaster`, and API routes | Preserve for this branch | Route migration requires redirects, saved-link analysis, and coordinated integration; visible labels and metadata migrate now. |\n\n## Validation plan\n\n1. Run the forbidden-language validator against production copy roots after each copy batch.\n2. Run copy-registry and feature tests after each owned surface changes.\n3. Run formatting, lint, typecheck, unit tests, language validation, build, and the isolated repository validation harness serially.\n4. Use only the language worktree's isolated validation database and its dedicated test server port; do not share Lanternwake's server, database, Playwright session, or generated build output.\n5. Before integration, fetch the completed Lanternwake branch and merge it into this branch; resolve conflicts in favor of Lanternwake structure and Voyagewright language.\n\n## Completion checklist\n\n- [x] Authoritative PDF located, read, checksum-recorded, and preserved in this worktree.\n- [x] Searchable Markdown companion created.\n- [x] Migration record and initial inventory structure created.\n- [x] Canonical glossary, ownership metadata, and feature copy catalogs created.\n- [ ] Production copy migration complete.\n- [ ] Forbidden-term validator and validation-pipeline integration complete.\n- [ ] All BLOCKER/HIGH inventory items resolved.\n- [ ] Full validation complete.\n- [ ] Lanternwake baseline synchronization complete.\n- [ ] Integration review requested; no automatic merge into Get-CanonicalDatabaseFamilyFingerprint {\r\n    $members = @(\r\n        foreach ($suffix in @(\"\", \"-wal\", \"-shm\", \"-journal\")) {\r\n            $memberPath = \"$canonicalDatabase$suffix\"\r\n            if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $memberPath -PathType Leaf) {\r\n                $memberItem = Get-Item -LiteralPath $memberPath\r\n                [pscustomobject]@{\r\n                    fileName = [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($memberPath)\r\n                    present = $true\r\n                    sha256 = (Get-FileHash -LiteralPath $memberPath -Algorithm SHA256).Hash.ToLowerInvariant()\r\n                    size = [long]$memberItem.Length\r\n                    mtimeIso = $memberItem.LastWriteTimeUtc.ToString(\"yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ\", bcrypt.hash(playerPassword, 12),\r\n      status: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n      claimedAt: new Date(),\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  for (const role of [\"CAPTAIN\", \"CREATOR\", \"PUBLISHER\"]) {\r\n    const existing = await db.platformRoleAssignment.findFirst({\r\n      where: { accountId: userId, role, scopeType: \"GLOBAL\", scopeId: null },\r\n    });\r\n    if (existing) {\r\n      await db.platformRoleAssignment.update({ where: { id: existing.id }, data: { revokedAt: null } });\r\n    } else {\r\n      await db.platformRoleAssignment.create({\r\n        data: { accountId: userId, role, scopeType: \"GLOBAL\", grantedBy: userId },\r\n      });\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const legacySessions = await db.taleSession.findMany({\r\n    where: { previewMode: false, memberships: { none: {} } },\r\n    include: { events: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } } },\r\n  });\r\n  for (const session of legacySessions) {\r\n    await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n      const placeholder = session.id,\r\n          playerProfileId: placeholder.id,\r\n          status: membershipStateForSession(session.status),\r\n          joinedAt: session.startedAt,\r\n          completedAt: session.completedAt,\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      await tx.taleSession.update({\r\n        where: { id: session.id },\r\n        data: {\r\n          voyageName: session.voyageName ?? } catch { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500 }\r\n    } while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline)\r\n    throw \"The application did not become ready at $Url within $Seconds seconds.\"\r\n}\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\r\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\r\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\r\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\r\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\r\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\r\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n\r\n## Animation development\r\n\r\nOpen `/dev/animations` or use the development-only `TEST ANIMATIONS` button. The lab contains all registered scenes, a no-API trailer, transport/scrub/speed/mode controls, StPageFlip book, live Rive input proof, Lottie controls, fallback states, and local FPS/long-task/runtime metrics. The route intentionally returns 404 from a production server.\r\n\r\nUse `npm run assets:validate` after any asset-contract change. Keep all runtime files and fallbacks under `public`, add provenance to `docs/asset-licenses.md`, and never use a remote CDN placeholder. See `docs/animation/showcase.md` for the manual pass and `docs/animation/` for scene/asset extension contracts.\r\n\r\n## Tall Tale Studio development\r\n\r\nThe seed creates `development-studio-voyage`, its editable draft, and published version 1.0 without replacing the original campaign seed. Sign in through Quartermaster and use Studio to edit it. Asset originals and generated derivatives live under ignored `.data/tall-tale-assets` by default. Override that location with an absolute `TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT`; set the upload ceiling with `TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`.\r\n\r\nDraft preview sessions are explicitly marked and may read draft assets only through an authenticated Creator session. Real Player playthroughs normally begin with a Captain invitation. The development catalog compatibility route remains available, but `/player/*`, `/captain/*`, and `/studio/*` are the canonical role workspaces. Every real playthrough pins itself to an immutable published version. Normal `--ensure` startup also adds missing platform roles/Player credentials and backfills memberships/reveal history for legacy Tall Tale sessions without resetting progress.\r\n\r\n## Clean-clone continuation\r\n\r\nAn authorized session on another computer needs only Git, Node/npm, and this repository. Clone `main`, copy `.env.example` or use the Windows launcher, then run `npm run validate`. No database, generated Prisma client, Playwright browser state, artifact, or machine path is required from the originating computer.\r\n\r\n## Troubleshooting\r\n\r\n- **Port 3000 is occupied:** stop that process or run `scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Port 3001`. The launcher never kills an unknown listener.\r\n- **Existing `.env` is incomplete:** the launcher preserves it and identifies each missing key. Add the key; it will not overwrite local secrets.\r\n- **Startup fails:** inspect `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development\\.forever\\logs`.\r\n- **Resetting disposable data is explicit:** `npm run db:preset -- awaiting-first-release` intentionally replaces the development fixture. Normal `npm run dev:stop` / `npm run dev:full` cycles preserve it. For validation, `npm run validate` always rebuilds `validation.db` from migrations.\r\n- **Browser binaries are missing:** `npm run validate` installs pinned Chromium and WebKit builds automatically.\r\n- **UNC/network checkout is slow:** the first mirror/install is expected to take longer; subsequent runs retain the dependency cache and only synchronize project files.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.8 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.7 seconds\nOutput:\n# Voyagewright Universal Language Migration Record\r\n\r\n## Status\r\n\r\n**Branch:** `development/universal-language`\r\n**Dedicated worktree:** `Forever-Treasure-Language`\r\n**Governing document:** `Voyagewright_Language_Design_Foundation.pdf` (Foundation v1.0, July 2026; SHA-256 `9D9668909CD448A9E471D0D62457498D0865CDF062D972D4612EB539FB40B46C`)\r\n**Starting committed baseline:** `7c3677035867081e4078536bef2f7d540bfd94e6` (`work/lanternwake-latest` remote state)\r\n**Implementation state:** In progress â€” glossary and copy architecture established; product-surface audit and migration follow.\r\n\r\n## Authority and change protection\r\n\r\nProject Lanternwake owns component and scene architecture, animation runtimes, scene hosts, lifecycle behavior, transitions, motion accessibility, and presentation mechanics. This branch owns visible product language only. Copy edits must preserve all existing behavior and animation hooks.\r\n\r\nThe active Lanternwake checkout is `forever-treasure-companion` on `work/lanternwake-latest` at `ec19f2115cca1a3de9dd44d7859f088485fcfb04`; it was not modified. A separate Phase 4 worktree contains concurrent uncommitted lifecycle changes in invitation, sign-in, voyage-room, and player-experience files. Static wording in those files is an integration-risk area and must be reconciled against their completed committed state before final integration.\r\n\r\n## Canonical terminology and capitalization\r\n\r\n| Concept                   | Canonical display term          |\r\n| ------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\r\n| Company                   | Absolute Relative Systems       |\r\n| Product                   | Voyagewright                    |\r\n| Creator interface         | Voyagewright Studio             |\r\n| Player interface          | Voyagewright Player             |\r\n| Live operator interface   | Captain's Console               |\r\n| Connector                 | Voyagewright Connector          |\r\n| Authored experience       | Chronicle                       |\r\n| Playthrough               | Voyage                          |\r\n| Operator                  | Captain                         |\r\n| Participants              | Crew                            |\r\n| Major division            | Chapter                         |\r\n| Authored unit             | Passage                         |\r\n| Progress point            | Waypoint                        |\r\n| Optional discovery        | Echo                            |\r\n| Major collected object    | Artifact                        |\r\n| Test playthrough          | Preview Voyage                  |\r\n| Completed record          | Voyage Record                   |\r\n| Creator collection        | Chronicle Library               |\r\n| Active-play collection    | Active Voyages                  |\r\n| Completed-play collection | Voyage History / Voyage Records |\r\n\r\nCapitalize canonical product objects when they name Voyagewright concepts. Use ordinary sentence case when the same words describe a non-product concept. Preserve literal names for ordinary controls such as Save, Cancel, Delete, Email, Password, Privacy, Version history, and Validation errors.\r\n\r\n## Language architecture\r\n\r\n| Artifact                          | Purpose                                                                            |\r\n| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `src/language/canonical-terms.ts` | Canonical terms, deprecated display terms, capitalization policy                   |\r\n| `src/language/copy-types.ts`      | Typed speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, voice layer, localization key |\r\n| `src/language/platform-copy.ts`   | Shared platform labels, empty/loading/recovery language                            |\r\n| `src/language/player-copy.ts`     | Functional-truth-first Player progression copy                                     |\r\n| `src/language/captain-copy.ts`    | Direct Captain control and consequence language                                    |\r\n| `src/language/studio-copy.ts`     | Creator-focused Studio labels                                                      |\r\n| `src/language/error-copy.ts`      | Parameterized, actionable error patterns                                           |\r\n\r\nThe product currently has no localization framework. Each new shared copy entry therefore carries a stable `localizationKey`, speaker ownership, audience, delivery context, and voice-layer metadata so localization can be introduced without using English copy as an identifier.\r\n\r\n## Audit coverage and application status\r\n\r\n| Surface                                  | Status      | Evidence / disposition                                                                                                                    |\r\n| ---------------------------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| Absolute Relative Systems public website | NOT PRESENT | No separate company-site package or deployment surface exists in this repository.                                                         |\r\n| Voyagewright marketing site              | IN PROGRESS | Root landing page and shared product shell are in `src/components/landing` and `src/components/shell`.                                    |\r\n| Voyagewright Player                      | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/player`, `src/components/player`, and `src/components/platform`.                                     |\r\n| Captain's Console                        | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/captain`, `src/components/captain`, `src/components/platform`.                                       |\r\n| Voyagewright Studio                      | IN PROGRESS | Routes and components under `src/app/studio`, `src/components/studio`, and `src/tall-tale`.                                               |\r\n| Voyagewright desktop wrapper             | NOT PRESENT | No Electron, Tauri, native wrapper, shortcut, or desktop manifest is tracked.                                                             |\r\n| Voyagewright Connector                   | NOT PRESENT | No connector application, tray surface, or capture-permission package is tracked.                                                         |\r\n| Installer / updater                      | NOT PRESENT | No installer or update configuration is tracked.                                                                                          |\r\n| Emails                                   | NOT PRESENT | No email template or delivery system is tracked.                                                                                          |\r\n| Notifications                            | PARTIAL     | In-app state and status notifications are audited with their owning Player/Captain flows. No separate OS notification service is tracked. |\r\n| Metadata and manifests                   | IN PROGRESS | Next metadata is in `src/app/layout.tsx`; no PWA manifest is tracked.                                                                     |\r\n| Documentation                            | IN PROGRESS | This record, foundation companion, inventory, and validator documentation are the owned documentation surfaces.                           |\r\n\r\n## Technical legacy and intentional preservation\r\n\r\n| Area                                                                    | Decision                 | Reason                                                                                                                         |\r\n| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\r\n| `campaignId`, `campaignSlug`, `storyBlock`, existing API route names    | Preserve internally      | Persisted data/API compatibility and low-risk migration boundary; must not appear in product-facing copy.                      |\r\n| `tall-tale` CSS/data identifiers and implementation file names          | Preserve internally      | Animation/style/runtime identifiers are Lanternwake-adjacent technical legacy, not product language.                           |\r\n| Chronicle-authored narration, dialogue, riddles, letters, and documents | Preserve                 | Chronicle authors own their voice; only accidental product-language leakage or placeholder copy is migrated.                   |\r\n| `/tales`, `/play`, `/tale`, `/quartermaster`, and API routes            | Preserve for this branch | Route migration requires redirects, saved-link analysis, and coordinated integration; visible labels and metadata migrate now. |\r\n\r\n## Validation plan\r\n\r\n1. Run the forbidden-language validator against production copy roots after each copy batch.\r\n2. Run copy-registry and feature tests after each owned surface changes.\r\n3. Run formatting, lint, typecheck, unit tests, language validation, build, and the isolated repository validation harness serially.\r\n4. Use only the language worktree's isolated validation database and its dedicated test server port; do not share Lanternwake's server, database, Playwright session, or generated build output.\r\n5. Before integration, fetch the completed Lanternwake branch and merge it into this branch; resolve conflicts in favor of Lanternwake structure and Voyagewright language.\r\n\r\n## Completion checklist\r\n\r\n- [x] Authoritative PDF located, read, checksum-recorded, and preserved in this worktree.\r\n- [x] Searchable Markdown companion created.\r\n- [x] Migration record and initial inventory structure created.\r\n- [x] Canonical glossary, ownership metadata, and feature copy catalogs created.\r\n- [ ] Production copy migration complete.\r\n- [ ] Forbidden-term validator and validation-pipeline integration complete.\r\n- [ ] All BLOCKER/HIGH inventory items resolved.\r\n- [ ] Full validation complete.\r\n- [ ] Lanternwake baseline synchronization complete.\r\n- [ ] ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-20T21:21:09.470Z

Implemented and committed the isolated Universal Language Overhaul.

- Worktree: `\\gwplastics.com\VT\Users\kgray\My Documents\treasurehunt\Forever-Treasure-Language`
- Branch: `development/universal-language`
- Start: `7c3677035867081e4078536bef2f7d540bfd94e6`
- End: `47e5e6d006ddd0aa96e3077af1d207bcbe38875c`
- Scope: 12 coherent commits; 115 files changed (+2181/−951); clean worktree.
- No merge, push, reset, stash, or modification of Lanternwake was performed.

Created the typed language system, feature copy catalogs, canonical terminology registry, automated legacy-language scanner, pipeline integration, accessibility/error copy, product-wide Player/Captain/Studio/API migration, and the copy inventory/record:

- [Migration record](<//US-VT-FS01/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/Forever-Treasure-Language/Development_Docs/Voyagewright_Universal_Language_Migration_Record.md>)
- [Copy inventory](<//US-VT-FS01/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/Forever-Treasure-Language/Development_Docs/Voyagewright_User_Facing_Copy_Inventory.csv>)
- [Language catalogs](<//US-VT-FS01/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/Forever-Treasure-Language/src/language>)
- [Legacy-language validator](<//US-VT-FS01/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/Forever-Treasure-Language/scripts/validate-user-facing-language.ts>)

Validation passed:

- Prettier check
- ESLint: zero errors; 19 nonblocking existing warnings
- TypeScript check
- Product-language validator
- 84 unit files / 790 tests, excluding the Lanternwake-owned `PlayerExperience` suite
- Production build
- Inventory integrity: 22 rows, zero pending and zero open BLOCKER/HIGH items

The full harness was attempted in a separate local runtime/database on ports 3100/3200. It stops at the existing Lanternwake `PlayerExperience` progression-scene suite (105/109 failures); that source and test have no semantic diff from the language baseline, so I left its architecture untouched. Browser acceptance, performance, and restart validation remain integration-gate work after Lanternwake resolves that failure.

Overlap/integration risks: Phase 4 currently has concurrent changes in the sign-in, invitation, voyage-room, PlayerExperience, TaleEditor, and validation-harness areas. No conflicts were resolved because no Lanternwake merge was performed. Final integration should merge completed Lanternwake first into a dedicated integration branch, then apply this branch’s copy changes.

Parallel work was split into Player (25 files), Captain (29), and Studio (23) lanes; all outputs were reviewed and serially committed.

Preflight recorded the canonical active worktree at `forever-treasure-companion` on `work/lanternwake-latest` (initially `ec19f21`; now concurrently advanced to `7c36770`). Its original untracked Foundation PDF remains its only observed change. The Phase 3, Phase 4, and newly present Phase 5 Lanternwake worktrees remain separate and untouched.
